title: Travel Phrases
description: Short phrases for transport, directions, and common travel moments.

cards.definition_en: An official document issued by a government that certifies the holder's identity and citizenship and allows them to travel internationally.
cards.example_sentence: I need to renew my passport before traveling.
cards.example_es: Necesito renovar mi pasaporte antes de viajar.
cards.example_en: I need to renew my passport before traveling.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a busy port where you hand over a pass, the Spanish pasaporte glows.

cards.definition_en: A document issued by an airline that allows a passenger to board a flight.
cards.example_sentence: I need to show my boarding pass before boarding the plane.
cards.example_es: Necesito presentar mi tarjeta de embarque antes de abordar el avión.
cards.example_en: I need to show my boarding pass before boarding the plane.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a wooden board holding a pass on a tarjeta, ready for the embarque flight.

cards.definition_en: A gate or area where passengers board a plane.
cards.example_sentence: The boarding gate is closed.
cards.example_es: La puerta de embarque está cerrada.
cards.example_en: The boarding gate is closed.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a bored gate (boarding gate) swinging open like a giant puerta for the airplane.

cards.definition_en: luggage that is checked in for transport on a flight.
cards.example_sentence: I'm going to check luggage before boarding the flight.
cards.example_es: Voy a facturar equipaje antes de abordar el vuelo.
cards.example_en: I'm going to check luggage before boarding the flight.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a CHEQUE stamped on a LUGGAGE cart as you check in your bags.

cards.definition_en: A bag that a passenger is allowed to bring onto an aircraft without checking it in.
cards.example_sentence: I'm going to bring my carry‑on bag onto the plane.
cards.example_es: Voy a llevar mi equipaje de mano al avión.
cards.example_en: I'm going to bring my carry‑on bag onto the plane.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a hand (mano) saying carry on while holding a bag.

cards.definition_en: A period of time by which something is late or postponed.
cards.example_sentence: The delay in delivery caused problems for the customers.
cards.example_es: El retraso en la entrega causó problemas a los clientes.
cards.example_en: The delay in delivery caused problems for the customers.
cards.mnemonic_en: A lazy 'de lay' cat drags a Spanish 'retraso' rope, making everything run late.

cards.definition_en: having been called off or terminated
cards.example_sentence: The flight was cancelled.
cards.example_es: El vuelo fue cancelado.
cards.example_en: The flight was cancelled.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a red stamp reading CANCELADO over the word cancelled

cards.definition_en: the act or process of arriving at a destination
cards.example_sentence: The arrival of the train was delayed by an hour.
cards.example_es: La llegada del tren se retrasó una hora.
cards.example_en: The arrival of the train was delayed by an hour.
cards.mnemonic_en: The train pulls in, and the big board flashes LLEGADA, signaling the arrival.

cards.definition_en: the act of leaving or the point at which someone or something departs.
cards.example_sentence: The departure of the train is scheduled for 8:00 a.m.
cards.example_es: La salida del tren está programada para las 8:00 a.m.
cards.example_en: The departure of the train is scheduled for 8:00 a.m.
cards.mnemonic_en: When the train leaves, the conductor shouts 'Salida!' and the departure begins.

cards.definition_en: The authority that regulates and taxes goods entering a country.
cards.example_sentence: The package arrived at customs without any problems.
cards.example_es: El paquete llegó a la aduana sin problemas.
cards.example_en: The package arrived at customs without any problems.
cards.mnemonic_en: Customs officer slapped an ADOANA stamp on my passport as I entered.

cards.definition_en: A procedure to verify safety or compliance, often involving inspection of items or people.
cards.example_sentence: The security check at the airport was very strict.
cards.example_es: El control de seguridad en el aeropuerto fue muy riguroso.
cards.example_en: The security check at the airport was very strict.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a guard saying 'Sure, I check' while holding a 'control' badge.

cards.definition_en: the act of flying through the air
cards.example_sentence: The flight tomorrow is delayed.
cards.example_es: El vuelo de mañana está retrasado.
cards.example_en: The flight tomorrow is delayed.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a bird taking a flight, shouting 'vuelo!' as it soars.

cards.definition_en: A piece of furniture or structure designed for sitting.
cards.example_sentence: The seat on the bus was very comfortable.
cards.example_es: El asiento del autobús estaba muy cómodo.
cards.example_en: The seat on the bus was very comfortable.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a comfy seat labeled 'A' for Asiento, the Spanish word for a place to sit.

cards.definition_en: A seat located by a window, typically on a train, plane, or theater, offering a view.
cards.example_sentence: I sat in the window seat of the train to enjoy the view.
cards.example_es: Me senté en la ventanilla del tren para disfrutar de la vista.
cards.example_en: I sat in the window seat of the train to enjoy the view.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a tiny vent in a plane; the window seat feels like a ventanilla you look through.

cards.definition_en: A seat located next to an aisle in a vehicle or theater.
cards.example_sentence: The aisle seat is comfortable.
cards.example_es: El asiento en el pasillo es cómodo.
cards.example_en: The aisle seat is comfortable.
cards.mnemonic_en: I pass low down the aisle to my seat.

cards.definition_en: to get on or into a vehicle, such as a plane or ship.
cards.example_sentence: The passenger must board before departure.
cards.example_es: El pasajero debe embarcar antes de la salida.
cards.example_en: The passenger must board before departure.
cards.mnemonic_en: A bored sailor finally gets to embarcar onto the ship.

cards.definition_en: to leave a vehicle or place, especially by disembarking
cards.example_sentence: Passengers must get off the bus before arriving at the stop.
cards.example_es: Los pasajeros deben desembarcar del autobús antes de llegar a la parada.
cards.example_en: Passengers must get off the bus before arriving at the stop.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a desert gate opening as you get off the boat, shouting 'Desembarcar!'

cards.definition_en: a flat surface used for displaying or serving items, often found in stores or restaurants.
cards.example_sentence: The counter at the café is full of cups.
cards.example_es: El mostrador del café está lleno de tazas.
cards.example_en: The counter at the café is full of cups.
cards.mnemonic_en: The counter displays the most rad door.

cards.definition_en: A place where passengers board or disembark from transportation, such as a bus or train station.
cards.example_sentence: The train arrives at the terminal at 5 p.m.
cards.example_es: El tren llega a la terminal a las 5 p.m.
cards.example_en: The train arrives at the terminal at 5 p.m.

cards.definition_en: A modification or alteration of a gate.
cards.example_sentence: The gate change was carried out yesterday.
cards.example_es: El cambio de puerta se realizó ayer.
cards.example_en: The gate change was carried out yesterday.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a gate that suddenly changes like a door flipping open.

cards.definition_en: A raised area where passengers wait for trains or buses.
cards.example_sentence: The train arrived at the platform and passengers began to disembark.
cards.example_es: El tren llegó al andén y los pasajeros comenzaron a bajar.
cards.example_en: The train arrived at the platform and passengers began to disembark.
cards.mnemonic_en: Think of a flat plate (plat) on a train's Andén, a perfect platform.

cards.definition_en: A plan that lists events or tasks and the times at which they will occur.
cards.example_sentence: The class schedule is posted every week.
cards.example_es: El horario de clases se publica cada semana.
cards.example_en: The class schedule is posted every week.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a jewel-shaped clock labeled schedule hanging on a Spanish horario board.

cards.definition_en: A rail vehicle that carries passengers or freight along a railway track.
cards.example_sentence: The train will arrive at the station at eight.
cards.example_es: El tren llegará a la estación a las ocho.
cards.example_en: The train will arrive at the station at eight.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a train shouting 'Tren!' as it whistles down the tracks.

cards.definition_en: A large motor vehicle that carries passengers by road, typically on a fixed route.
cards.example_sentence: The bus arrives at eight.
cards.example_es: El autobús llega a las ocho.
cards.example_en: The bus arrives at eight.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a bright yellow bus labeled autobús zooming down the road.

cards.definition_en: A designated place where buses pick up and drop off passengers.
cards.example_sentence: I'm going to the bus stop to catch the bus.
cards.example_es: Voy a la parada de autobús para coger el bus.
cards.example_en: I'm going to the bus stop to catch the bus.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a bus pulling up at a bright parada sign and stopping.

cards.definition_en: A vehicle for hire that transports passengers for a fare.
cards.example_sentence: I need a taxi to get to the airport.
cards.example_es: Necesito un taxi para ir al aeropuerto.
cards.example_en: I need a taxi to get to the airport.

cards.definition_en: An underground railway system used for urban transit.
cards.example_sentence: I take the subway every morning to get to work.
cards.example_es: Voy al metro cada mañana para ir al trabajo.
cards.example_en: I take the subway every morning to get to work.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a submarine (sub) navigating a metro tunnel as the city’s underground way.

cards.definition_en: A journey that starts and ends at the same place.
cards.example_sentence: I bought a round trip ticket for my trip to Madrid.
cards.example_es: Compré un billete de ida y vuelta para mi viaje a Madrid.
cards.example_en: I bought a round trip ticket for my trip to Madrid.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a round circle (ronda) that trips you back home, like an ida y vuelta.

cards.definition_en: A ticket that allows travel in only one direction, not a return.
cards.example_sentence: The price of a one-way ticket is cheaper than that of a round-trip ticket.
cards.example_es: El precio del solo ida es más barato que el del ida y vuelta.
cards.example_en: The price of a one-way ticket is cheaper than that of a round-trip ticket.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a solo rider saying ida as he hands over a one-way ticket.

cards.definition_en: the act of reserving something in advance, or a booking made for a future use
cards.example_sentence: I made a reservation for two people.
cards.example_es: He hecho una reserva para dos personas.
cards.example_en: I made a reservation for two people.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a waiter noting a reservation on a wine reserva list.

cards.definition_en: A diagram that represents an area, showing features such as roads, borders, or terrain.
cards.example_sentence: The treasure map is hidden under the table.
cards.example_es: El mapa del tesoro está escondido bajo la mesa.
cards.example_en: The treasure map is hidden under the table.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a MAP that looks like a MAPA poster of the world.

cards.definition_en: Indicates a location to the left side.
collocations: on the left side of the room
cards.example_sentence: On the left of the store, you'll find the park.
cards.example_es: A la izquierda de la tienda, encontrarás el parque.
cards.example_en: On the left of the store, you'll find the park.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a sign pointing left that says A la izquierda and the arrow says On the left.

cards.definition_en: Located or positioned to the right side.
main_translations_es: a la derecha de la calle
collocations: on the right side of the road
on the right side of the page
cards.example_sentence: Keep going on the right until you reach the park.
cards.example_es: Sigue a la derecha hasta el parque.
cards.example_en: Keep going on the right until you reach the park.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a hand shouting de‑reach‑a while pointing on the right.

cards.definition_en: Straight ahead means directly forward, in a straight line.
cards.example_sentence: Keep going straight ahead until the intersection.
cards.example_es: Sigue todo recto hasta el cruce.
cards.example_en: Keep going straight ahead until the intersection.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a straight road with a sign that reads recto, pointing straight ahead

cards.definition_en: a point where two lines or surfaces meet, forming an angle.
cards.example_sentence: The cat hid in the corner of the hallway.
cards.example_es: El gato se escondió en la esquina del pasillo.
cards.example_en: The cat hid in the corner of the hallway.
cards.mnemonic_en: A corn ear rests on the esquina, marking the corner.

cards.definition_en: At or within a short distance.
cards.example_sentence: The park is near my house.
cards.example_es: El parque está cerca de mi casa.
cards.example_en: The park is near my house.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a circus near the arena and the ringmaster yells 'cerca' to signal they're close.

cards.definition_en: at a great distance from a particular point or place.
cards.example_sentence: The lighthouse is far from the coast.
cards.example_es: El faro está lejos de la costa.
cards.example_en: The lighthouse is far from the coast.
cards.mnemonic_en: A far horizon feels like the Spanish word lejos stretching into the distance.

cards.definition_en: A place where one can cross from one side to another.
cards.example_sentence: The crossing of the street is signaled with flashing lights.
cards.example_es: El cruce de la calle está señalizado con luces intermitentes.
cards.example_en: The crossing of the street is signaled with flashing lights.
cards.mnemonic_en: Crossing sounds like cruise—picture a cruise ship crossing a busy street.

cards.definition_en: A device that controls traffic by displaying red, yellow, and green lights.
cards.example_sentence: The traffic light turned red and the drivers stopped.
cards.example_es: El semáforo se puso rojo y los conductores se detuvieron.
cards.example_en: The traffic light turned red and the drivers stopped.
cards.mnemonic_en: The traffic light flashes red, yellow, green like a bright semáforo guiding cars.

cards.definition_en: A public road in a city or town, typically with buildings on one or both sides.
cards.example_sentence: The street is full of people.
cards.example_es: La calle está llena de gente.
cards.example_en: The street is full of people.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a street where every house has a big 'C' on the door, reminding you of the Spanish 'calle'.

cards.definition_en: A wide street, especially one that is lined with trees or buildings.
cards.example_sentence: The main avenue is full of shops.
cards.example_es: La Avenida principal está llena de tiendas.
cards.example_en: The main avenue is full of shops.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture an AVENUE where every sign says A VENIDA, inviting you to come and go.

cards.definition_en: A public open space surrounded by buildings, often used for gathering or commerce.
cards.example_sentence: The square of the town is full of people.
cards.example_es: La plaza del pueblo está llena de gente.
cards.example_en: The square of the town is full of people.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a town square bustling like a Spanish plaza.

cards.definition_en: a movement or rotation around an axis
cards.example_sentence: I turn the steering wheel to change lanes.
cards.example_es: Giro el volante para cambiar de carril.
cards.example_en: I turn the steering wheel to change lanes.
cards.mnemonic_en: Turn the knob and picture a fast giro spin

cards.definition_en: the central business district of a city, typically characterized by high-rise buildings and commercial activity.
cards.example_sentence: I like walking around downtown.
cards.example_es: Me gusta caminar por el centro de la ciudad.
cards.example_en: I like walking around downtown.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a bustling downtown as the city's centro, the central town hub.

cards.definition_en: the details of a place where someone lives or works.
cards.example_sentence: What is your address?
cards.example_es: ¿Cuál es tu dirección?
cards.example_en: What is your address?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a dress with a house tag on it to remember an address.

cards.definition_en: a building where people pay to stay for a short time
cards.example_sentence: The hotel is near the city center.
cards.example_es: El hotel está cerca del centro.
cards.example_en: The hotel is near the city center.

cards.definition_en: the act of receiving guests or visitors, especially at an entrance
cards.example_sentence: The hotel's reception is open 24 hours a day.
cards.example_es: La recepción del hotel está abierta las 24 horas.
cards.example_en: The hotel's reception is open 24 hours a day.
cards.mnemonic_en: At the hotel reception I picture a receptionist saying recepción as guests arrive.

cards.definition_en: A separate area within a building used for a particular purpose.
cards.example_sentence: The room is clean and tidy.
cards.example_es: La habitación está limpia y ordenada.
cards.example_en: The room is clean and tidy.
cards.mnemonic_en: A room feels like a habitat—habitación, a cozy den.

cards.definition_en: A small metal instrument used to open locks or secure items.
cards.example_sentence: The key to the door is in the drawer.
cards.example_es: La llave de la puerta está en el cajón.
cards.example_en: The key to the door is in the drawer.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a key turning the Spanish llave lock on a treasure chest.

cards.definition_en: A piece of absorbent cloth or paper used for drying or wiping.
cards.example_sentence: I need a clean towel.
cards.example_es: Necesito una toalla limpia.
cards.example_en: I need a clean towel.
cards.mnemonic_en: I picture a towel that looks like a giant toalla waving in the wind

cards.definition_en: A room in a house or apartment equipped for washing and personal hygiene.
cards.example_sentence: I'm going to the bathroom before leaving.
cards.example_es: Voy al baño antes de salir.
cards.example_en: I'm going to the bathroom before leaving.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a bathroom with a giant "baño" bathtub overflowing with bubbles

cards.definition_en: A bed that is larger than a single bed, typically accommodating two people.
cards.example_sentence: The room has a double bed so guests feel comfortable.
cards.example_es: La habitación tiene una cama doble para que los huéspedes se sientan cómodos.
cards.example_en: The room has a double bed so guests feel comfortable.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine two beds side by side, a double bed, echoing the Spanish doble

cards.definition_en: A single night, a period of 24 hours.
cards.example_sentence: One night, the cat hid under the bed.
cards.example_es: Una noche, el gato se escondió bajo la cama.
cards.example_en: One night, the cat hid under the bed.
cards.mnemonic_en: See a single night sky whispering 'one night' like the Spanish 'una noche'

cards.definition_en: to register upon arrival, typically at a hotel or event
collocations: check in at the front desk
cards.example_sentence: To complete the process, you must check in on the website.
cards.example_es: Para completar el proceso, debes registrarte en la página web.
cards.example_en: To complete the process, you must check in on the website.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a clerk handing you a check as you register at the front desk

cards.definition_en: to leave a hotel after paying the bill
cards.example_sentence: The check out from the hotel was smooth.
cards.example_es: La salida del hotel fue sin contratiempos.
cards.example_en: The check out from the hotel was smooth.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a giant checkmark being taken out the hotel door as you exit.

cards.definition_en: A phrase indicating that breakfast is included in the price or package.
cards.example_sentence: The price includes breakfast included in the room.
cards.example_es: El precio incluye desayuno incluido en la habitación.
cards.example_en: The price includes breakfast included in the room.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a break-fast banner that says incluido, so the meal is already included.

cards.definition_en: The process of controlling the temperature, humidity, and air quality in a building or vehicle.
cards.example_sentence: The air conditioning in the hotel is very efficient.
cards.example_es: El aire acondicionado en el hotel es muy eficiente.
cards.example_en: The air conditioning in the hotel is very efficient.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a chilly wind that airs and condi-tiones the room like a Spanish acondicionado.

cards.definition_en: a wireless networking technology that allows devices to connect to the internet or communicate with each other without cables.
cards.example_sentence: I connected my phone to the hotel’s Wi‑Fi.
cards.example_es: Conecté mi teléfono al Wi‑Fi del hotel.
cards.example_en: I connected my phone to the hotel’s Wi‑Fi.

cards.definition_en: A mechanical device that transports people or goods between floors of a building.
cards.example_sentence: The elevator is at the building's entrance.
cards.example_es: El ascensor está en la entrada del edificio.
cards.example_en: The elevator is at the building's entrance.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture an elevator shouting Eleva señor as it lifts you up.

cards.definition_en: Items of personal belongings carried in a suitcase or bag, especially when traveling.
cards.example_sentence: My luggage from the flight arrived late.
cards.example_es: El equipaje de mi vuelo llegó tarde.
cards.example_en: My luggage from the flight arrived late.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a strong lug pulling a suitcase full of equipaje across the airport.

cards.definition_en: A bag carried on the back, typically with two straps, used for carrying books, supplies, or other items.
cards.example_sentence: I need to bring my backpack to campus.
cards.example_es: Necesito llevar mi mochila al campus.
cards.example_en: I need to bring my backpack to campus.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a mochi bag strapped to your back, a mochila backpack.

cards.definition_en: a device that supplies electrical energy to recharge a battery
cards.example_sentence: I need a new charger for my phone.
cards.example_es: Necesito un cargador nuevo para mi teléfono.
cards.example_en: I need a new charger for my phone.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a charger feeding a car's battery, like a CARGA‑door opening power.

cards.definition_en: a device that connects an electrical appliance to a power source
cards.example_sentence: The plug in the wall is loose.
cards.example_es: El enchufe en la pared está suelto.
cards.example_en: The plug in the wall is loose.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a bright plug stamped ENCHUFE, snapping into the wall like a Spanish power plug

cards.definition_en: A device that allows one type of connection to be used with another.
cards.example_sentence: I need an adapter to plug my laptop into the European outlet.
cards.example_es: Necesito un adaptador para conectar mi portátil al enchufe europeo.
cards.example_en: I need an adapter to plug my laptop into the European outlet.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize an adapter plugging a Spanish adaptador into a socket, the words sound identical.

cards.definition_en: Money in the form of coins and banknotes that can be used for transactions.
cards.example_sentence: The ATM gave me cash.
cards.example_es: El cajero me dio dinero en efectivo.
cards.example_en: The ATM gave me cash.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a cash box, a caja overflowing with dinero.

cards.definition_en: A small, flat piece of paper, cardboard, or plastic used for identification, payment, or as a playing piece.
cards.example_sentence: I bought a credit card at the bank.
cards.example_es: Compré una tarjeta de crédito en el banco.
cards.example_en: I bought a credit card at the bank.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a playing card covered in dark tar with a tiny jet sticker echoing tarjeta

cards.definition_en: A small, flat piece of metal used as money.
cards.example_sentence: I bought a gold coin at the fair.
cards.example_es: Compré una moneda de oro en la feria.
cards.example_en: I bought a gold coin at the fair.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a shiny coin stamped with the word moneda on its edge.

cards.definition_en: the act of becoming different
cards.example_sentence: Change is inevitable.
cards.example_es: El cambio es inevitable.
cards.example_en: Change is inevitable.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a coin flipping and you hear cambio as it changes.

cards.definition_en: A written acknowledgment of having received something, typically a payment or goods.
cards.example_sentence: Keep the receipt in your folder.
cards.example_es: Guarda el recibo en tu carpeta.
cards.example_en: Keep the receipt in your folder.
cards.mnemonic_en: I hand the cashier a receipt, thinking of my recibo tucked in my pocket.

cards.definition_en: the amount of money expected, required, or given in payment for something.
cards.example_sentence: The price of the ticket is twenty euros.
cards.example_es: El precio de la entrada es de veinte euros.
cards.example_en: The price of the ticket is twenty euros.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a price tag shouting precio as it slides down a cash register.

cards.definition_en: Not closed; allowing passage or access.
cards.example_sentence: The restaurant is open every day.
cards.example_es: El restaurante está abierto todos los días.
cards.example_en: The restaurant is open every day.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine an open door shouting abierto as you walk in.

cards.definition_en: Not open; shut.
cards.example_sentence: The museum is closed.
cards.example_es: El museo está cerrado.
cards.example_en: The museum is closed.
cards.mnemonic_en: Closed sounds like Spanish 'cerrado', picture a door shut with a big red 'C' lock.

cards.definition_en: Assistance or support given to someone in need.
cards.example_sentence: I need help.
cards.example_es: Necesito ayuda.
cards.example_en: I need help.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a superhero shouting 'Ayuda!' as he lifts a heavy box, reminding you that help sounds like ayuda.

cards.definition_en: to contact the police for assistance or to report a crime
main_translations_es: Ponte en contacto con la policía
collocations: call the police if there's a fire
call the police for a robbery
call the police to report a crime
cards.example_sentence: In case of emergency, call the police.
cards.example_es: En caso de emergencia, llama a la policía.
cards.example_en: In case of emergency, call the police.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a llama dialing the phone to call the police.

cards.definition_en: A statement expressing the speaker's requirement for a medical professional.
collocations: I need a doctor right now
I need a doctor for my child
I need a doctor at the hospital
cards.example_sentence: I need a doctor to check my headache.
cards.example_es: Necesito un médico para revisar mi dolor de cabeza.
cards.example_en: I need a doctor to check my headache.
cards.mnemonic_en: When I shout 'I need a doctor', I picture saying 'necesito doctor' to a panicked patient.

cards.definition_en: A sudden, urgent situation requiring immediate action.
cards.example_sentence: The medical emergency requires immediate attention.
cards.example_es: La emergencia médica requiere atención inmediata.
cards.example_en: The medical emergency requires immediate attention.
cards.mnemonic_en: A flashing red alarm makes me think emergency, like the word emerges from the chaos

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that the speaker has lost their passport.
main_translations_es: He perdido mi documento de viaje
collocations: I lost my passport at the airport
I lost my passport while traveling
I lost my passport and had to get a replacement
I lost my passport and can't travel
cards.example_sentence: I lost my passport, so I need to apply for a new one.
cards.example_es: He perdido mi pasaporte, así que necesito solicitar uno nuevo.
cards.example_en: I lost my passport, so I need to apply for a new one.
cards.mnemonic_en: I lost my passport, just like I lost my 'pasaporte' on a windy beach.

cards.definition_en: A sentence expressing that the speaker has misplaced or lost their wallet.
collocations: I lost my wallet at the restaurant
I lost my wallet in the subway
I lost my wallet and had to cancel my cards
cards.example_sentence: I lost my wallet on the bus.
cards.example_es: He perdido mi cartera en el autobús.
cards.example_en: I lost my wallet on the bus.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a carter dropping his wallet on the road, shouting lost my wallet!

cards.definition_en: I am ill or unwell.
collocations: I am sick with a cold
I am sick and need to rest
I am sick to my stomach
cards.example_sentence: I am sick and can't go to work.
cards.example_es: Estoy enfermo y no puedo ir al trabajo.
cards.example_en: I am sick and can't go to work.
cards.mnemonic_en: I am sick, picture a patient in a hospital shouting 'Estoy enfermo' while clutching his stomach.

cards.definition_en: It is not functioning properly; it has been damaged.
cards.example_sentence: The glass is broken.
cards.example_es: El vaso está roto.
cards.example_en: The glass is broken.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a broken vase yelling Roto!

cards.definition_en: A phrase indicating that something is not functioning or operating as intended.
collocations: It does not work for me
It does not work on my machine
It does not work as expected
cards.example_sentence: The button I tried to press does not work.
cards.example_es: No funciona el botón que intenté presionar.
cards.example_en: The button I tried to press does not work.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a broken robot shouting No function, it does not work.

cards.definition_en: A polite request asking someone to say something again.
collocations: Can you repeat the last part?
cards.example_sentence: Can you repeat what you said?
cards.example_es: ¿Puede repetir lo que dijo?
cards.example_en: Can you repeat what you said?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a can shouting Can you repeat? to a parrot that answers repetir.

cards.definition_en: A polite request to reduce the speed.
collocations: Could you speak more slowly, please?
Could you explain more slowly, please?
cards.example_sentence: Could you speak more slowly, please?
cards.example_es: Podrías hablar más despacio, por favor?
cards.example_en: Could you speak more slowly, please?
cards.mnemonic_en: A massive pushy robot crawls slower, politely saying please.

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating lack of comprehension.
collocations: I do not understand what you mean
I do not understand the instructions
I do not understand the concept
cards.example_sentence: I do not understand what you are saying.
cards.example_es: No entiendo lo que dices.
cards.example_en: I do not understand what you are saying.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a person shaking head saying no while saying I do not understand.

cards.definition_en: A question asking whether someone can speak English.
cards.example_sentence: Do you speak English?
cards.example_es: ¿Habla inglés?
cards.example_en: Do you speak English?
cards.mnemonic_en: A talking dog (habla) asks, Do you speak English?

cards.definition_en: A question asking for the location where water can be purchased.
collocations: Where can I buy water in the city?
Where can I buy water near me?
Where can I buy water at the store?
cards.example_sentence: Where can I buy water?
cards.example_es: ¿Dónde puedo comprar agua?
cards.example_en: Where can I buy water?
cards.mnemonic_en: A mafia don hands you water, so you know where to buy water.

cards.definition_en: A question asking for the duration of an event or process.
collocations: How long does it take to finish?
How long does it take to get there?
How long does it take to learn?
cards.example_sentence: How long does it take?
cards.example_es: ¿Cuánto tarda?
cards.example_en: How long does it take?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a tardy snail asking, How long does it take?

cards.definition_en: A question asking for the time at which something departs.
collocations: I need to know what time does it leave?
Do you know what time does it leave?
Can you tell me what time does it leave?
cards.example_sentence: What time does the train leave?
cards.example_es: ¿A qué hora sale el tren?
cards.example_en: What time does the train leave?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a clock shouting What time does it leave while a train says a que hora sale

cards.definition_en: A question asking for the time of arrival.
collocations: What time does it arrive at the station?
What time does it arrive on schedule?
synonyms_en: At what time does it arrive?
cards.example_sentence: What time does the train arrive?
cards.example_es: ¿A qué hora llega el tren?
cards.example_en: What time does the train arrive?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a clock shouting What time as a bus pulls up, reminding you of a qué hora llega.

cards.definition_en: A question asking whether a bathroom is available.
collocations: Is there a bathroom in this building?
Is there a bathroom on the second floor?
Is there a bathroom in the restaurant?
cards.example_sentence: Is there a bathroom nearby?
cards.example_es: ¿Hay un baño cerca de aquí?
cards.example_en: Is there a bathroom nearby?
cards.mnemonic_en: Hey, a bathroom?

cards.definition_en: A question asking if card payment is accepted.
synonyms_en: Can I pay with a card?
cards.example_sentence: Can I pay by card?
cards.example_es: ¿Puedo pagar con tarjeta?
cards.example_en: Can I pay by card?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a car asking, Can I pay by card? while you hand it a tarjeta.

cards.english: Do you have a room available?
cards.definition_en: A question asking whether a room is free for use.
collocations: Do you have a room available for tonight?
Do you have a room available for a short stay?
Do you have a room available for a conference?
Do you have a room available next week?
cards.example_sentence: Do you have a room available at your hotel?
cards.example_es: ¿Tiene una habitación libre en su hotel?
cards.example_en: Do you have a room available at your hotel?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a library asking for a free room: Do you have a room available?

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that the speaker requires a booking.
collocations: I need a reservation for two
I need a reservation for tonight
I need a reservation for a table
cards.example_sentence: I need a reservation for dinner tomorrow.
cards.example_es: Necesito una reserva para cenar mañana.
cards.example_en: I need a reservation for dinner tomorrow.
cards.mnemonic_en: I need a reservation, picture me yelling 'Necesito una reserva' to the host.

cards.definition_en: I am currently away from work or home for leisure.
cards.example_sentence: I am on vacation at the beach.
cards.example_es: Estoy de vacaciones en la playa.
cards.example_en: I am on vacation at the beach.
cards.mnemonic_en: I lounge on a beach, staying like estoy on vacation.

cards.definition_en: A trip taken for work purposes.
cards.example_sentence: I have to pack for the business trip tomorrow.
cards.example_es: Tengo que preparar mi equipaje para el viaje de trabajo mañana.
cards.example_en: I have to pack for the business trip tomorrow.
cards.mnemonic_en: A busy bee (biz-ness) flies on a work trip.

cards.definition_en: Unfavorable atmospheric conditions.
cards.example_sentence: The bad weather prevented us from going to the beach.
cards.example_es: El mal tiempo nos impidió salir a la playa.
cards.example_en: The bad weather prevented us from going to the beach.
cards.mnemonic_en: Bad weather is a mal (bad) tempo that drowns the dance floor

cards.definition_en: Having or showing a bright, warm, and clear sky.
cards.example_sentence: The day is sunny and perfect for a picnic.
cards.example_es: El día está soleado y perfecto para un picnic.
cards.example_en: The day is sunny and perfect for a picnic.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a bright sol shining, making everything sunny.

cards.definition_en: characterized by frequent rain
cards.example_sentence: The day is rainy.
cards.example_es: El día está lluvioso.
cards.example_en: The day is rainy.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a rainy street where each drop whispers lluvia as it falls.

cards.definition_en: A boat or ship used to carry people or goods across a body of water.
cards.example_sentence: The ferry arrives at 10 in the morning.
cards.example_es: El ferry llega a las 10 de la mañana.
cards.example_en: The ferry arrives at 10 in the morning.

cards.definition_en: A small vessel used for traveling on water.
cards.example_sentence: The boat sails calmly across the sea.
cards.example_es: El barco navega tranquilamente por el mar.
cards.example_en: The boat sails calmly across the sea.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a boat with a big bar of chocolate on deck, like a barco.

cards.definition_en: A sandy area by the sea where people can swim and sunbathe.
cards.example_sentence: I love walking along the beach at sunset.
cards.example_es: Me encanta caminar por la playa al atardecer.
cards.example_en: I love walking along the beach at sunset.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a beach where every wave whispers "play-a" like a Spanish song

cards.definition_en: A large natural elevation of the earth's surface, typically rising abruptly and reaching a peak.
cards.example_sentence: The mountain rises majestically over the valley.
cards.example_es: La montaña se alza majestuosa sobre el valle.
cards.example_en: The mountain rises majestically over the valley.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a massive mount of cheese topped with a Spanish montaña flag soaring like a mountain.

title: Daily Life
description: Useful vocabulary for routines, places, and simple actions.

cards.definition_en: to stop sleeping and become awake
cards.example_sentence: I wake up at seven in the morning.
cards.example_es: Me despierto a las siete de la mañana.
cards.example_en: I wake up at seven in the morning.
cards.mnemonic_en: The alarm bell says wake up while a sleepy llama named Despertar yawns.

cards.definition_en: To wash oneself by standing under a spray of water.
collocations: take a shower in the morning
cards.example_sentence: I need to take a shower after the workout.
cards.example_es: Necesito ducharme después del entrenamiento.
cards.example_en: I need to take a shower after the workout.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture ducks in a bathroom, because 'ducharse' sounds like 'ducks are' taking a shower

cards.definition_en: The first meal of the day, typically eaten in the morning.
cards.example_sentence: I have breakfast at eight every morning.
cards.example_es: Desayuno a las ocho cada mañana.
cards.example_en: I have breakfast at eight every morning.
cards.mnemonic_en: I break fast at sunrise, the day soon starts with a tasty desayuno

cards.definition_en: To clean one's teeth using a toothbrush.
collocations: brush your teeth twice a day
cards.example_sentence: Remember to brush your teeth before going to bed.
cards.example_es: Recuerda cepillarse los dientes antes de acostarte.
cards.example_en: Remember to brush your teeth before going to bed.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a sea (cep) of bristles brushing your teeth.

cards.definition_en: To put on clothing.
cards.example_sentence: I got dressed quickly before leaving.
cards.example_es: Me vestí rápidamente antes de salir.
cards.example_en: I got dressed quickly before leaving.
cards.mnemonic_en: A vest dresses the doll, so remember vestirse means get dressed

cards.definition_en: To go out of one's house.
collocations: leave home and never look back
leave home to start a new life
cards.example_sentence: I have to leave home early tomorrow.
cards.example_es: Tengo que salir de casa temprano mañana.
cards.example_en: I have to leave home early tomorrow.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a levitating house as you leave home, linking 'lev' to 'salir' and 'home' to 'casa'.

cards.definition_en: To reach a destination at the scheduled or expected time.
collocations: arrive on time for the interview
make sure to arrive on time
arrive on time and be ready
cards.example_sentence: It's important to arrive on time for the interview.
cards.example_es: Es importante llegar a tiempo a la entrevista.
cards.example_en: It's important to arrive on time for the interview.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a bus arriving arriba on time, perfectly punctual like a clock.

cards.definition_en: occurring or arriving after the expected or usual time.
cards.example_sentence: He arrived late to the meeting.
cards.example_es: Llegó tarde a la reunión.
cards.example_en: He arrived late to the meeting.
cards.mnemonic_en: Being tardy feels like saying tarde as you walk in late.

cards.definition_en: occurring or done before the usual or expected time
cards.example_sentence: He arrived early to the meeting.
cards.example_es: Llegó temprano a la reunión.
cards.example_en: He arrived early to the meeting.
cards.mnemonic_en: The king (el rey) rises early, just like a temprano sunrise.

cards.definition_en: A meal eaten around midday.
cards.example_sentence: I have lunch at one o'clock in the afternoon.
cards.example_es: Yo almuerzo a la una de la tarde.
cards.example_en: I have lunch at one o'clock in the afternoon.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a lunch of almonds on a big table, because almuerzo starts with “alm” like almond.

cards.definition_en: the main meal of the evening
cards.example_sentence: Dinner is ready.
cards.example_es: La cena está lista.
cards.example_en: Dinner is ready.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a dinner scene where the family says cena before eating.

cards.definition_en: To prepare food by heating it.
cards.example_sentence: I like to cook Italian dishes on weekends.
cards.example_es: Me gusta cocinar platos italianos los fines de semana.
cards.example_en: I like to cook Italian dishes on weekends.
cards.mnemonic_en: A cook shouts Coco while stirring the pot.

cards.definition_en: To remove dirt or impurities from something.
cards.example_sentence: I will clean the house before the party.
cards.example_es: Voy a limpiar la casa antes de la fiesta.
cards.example_en: I will clean the house before the party.
cards.mnemonic_en: Clean: picture a limp ear being wiped spotless.

cards.definition_en: To clean the dishes by rinsing them with water and soap.
collocations: I have to wash the dishes
She hates to wash the dishes
We wash the dishes after dinner
cards.example_sentence: I am going to wash the dishes after dinner.
cards.example_es: Voy a lavar los platos después de la cena.
cards.example_en: I am going to wash the dishes after dinner.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a lava river washing the dishes clean.

cards.definition_en: To arrange the sheets, blankets, and pillows on a bed after getting up.
cards.example_sentence: Every morning, my brother always gets up early to make the bed before going to school.
cards.example_es: Cada mañana, mi hermano siempre se levanta temprano para hacer la cama antes de ir a la escuela.
cards.example_en: Every morning, my brother always gets up early to make the bed before going to school.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture yourself hammering a Spanish cama, turning it into a perfect make‑the‑bed.

cards.definition_en: A room used for sleeping.
cards.example_sentence: The bedroom is decorated with soft colors.
cards.example_es: El dormitorio está decorado con colores suaves.
cards.example_en: The bedroom is decorated with soft colors.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a dormitory full of beds, just like a bedroom.

cards.definition_en: A room or area where food is prepared and cooked.
cards.example_sentence: The kitchen is freshly painted.
cards.example_es: La cocina está recién pintada.
cards.example_en: The kitchen is freshly painted.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a kitchen where a big coconut (coco) is cooking, reminding you of cocina.

cards.definition_en: A room in a house used for relaxing and entertaining guests.
cards.example_sentence: The living room is decorated with a blue sofa.
cards.example_es: La sala de estar está decorada con un sofá azul.
cards.example_en: The living room is decorated with a blue sofa.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a lively salsa party in the living room.

cards.definition_en: A room containing a toilet and sink for personal hygiene.
cards.example_sentence: Where is the bathroom?
cards.example_es: ¿Dónde está el baño?
cards.example_en: Where is the bathroom?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a bathroom sign that reads baño, reminding you the bath is the bathroom.

cards.definition_en: A movable barrier that provides access to or from a building, room, or vehicle.
cards.example_sentence: I closed the door before leaving.
cards.example_es: Cerré la puerta antes de salir.
cards.example_en: I closed the door before leaving.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a door with a big sign that reads PUERTA.

cards.definition_en: A framed opening in a wall or building that lets in light and air, often fitted with glass.
cards.example_sentence: The window is open.
cards.example_es: La ventana está abierta.
cards.example_en: The window is open.
cards.mnemonic_en: A vent in the house opens a window, reminding me of the Spanish ventana.

cards.definition_en: A piece of furniture with a seat, back, and legs for one person to sit on.
cards.example_sentence: The chair is next to the table.
cards.example_es: La silla está al lado de la mesa.
cards.example_en: The chair is next to the table.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a giant silla turned into a comfy chair that you share with friends.

cards.definition_en: A piece of furniture with a flat top and legs, used for working, eating, or placing items.
cards.example_sentence: The table is covered with books.
cards.example_es: La mesa está cubierta de libros.
cards.example_en: The table is covered with books.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a Spanish mesa holding a dinner table of tacos.

cards.definition_en: Objects used to open locks or to operate a keyboard.
cards.example_sentence: I can't find my keys, have you seen them?
cards.example_es: No encuentro mis llaves, ¿las has visto?
cards.example_en: I can't find my keys, have you seen them?
cards.mnemonic_en: She jingles the keys, tiny llaves, unlocking a bright treasure chest.

cards.definition_en: A device used for voice communication over distances.
cards.example_sentence: Can you pass me the phone, please?
cards.example_es: ¿Puedes pasarme el teléfono, por favor?
cards.example_en: Can you pass me the phone, please?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a phone morphing into a huge teléfono with a bright pho label.

cards.definition_en: A device used to replenish the electrical power of a battery or other rechargeable device.
cards.example_sentence: Where is my phone's charger?
cards.example_es: ¿Dónde está el cargador de mi teléfono?
cards.example_en: Where is my phone's charger?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a charger loading a car, because charger sounds like Spanish cargar, to load.

cards.definition_en: Items worn to cover the body.
cards.example_sentence: I bought new clothes for the trip.
cards.example_es: Compré ropa nueva para el viaje.
cards.example_en: I bought new clothes for the trip.
cards.mnemonic_en: I close the wardrobe door and pull out my bright ropa, the clothes I love.

cards.definition_en: Footwear that covers and protects the feet, typically consisting of a sole and a heel.
cards.example_sentence: I bought new shoes for the wedding.
cards.example_es: Me compré unos zapatos nuevos para la boda.
cards.example_en: I bought new shoes for the wedding.
cards.mnemonic_en: I zap my toes with shiny new shoes.

cards.definition_en: A piece of clothing worn on the feet, usually covering the ankle and sometimes extending up the leg.
cards.example_sentence: My socks are wet after the rain.
cards.example_es: Mis calcetines están mojados después de la lluvia.
cards.example_en: My socks are wet after the rain.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a sock hugging a calf—calcetín—so you remember socks.

cards.definition_en: A household appliance used to wash laundry.
collocations: wash clothes in the washing machine
cards.example_sentence: I bought a new washing machine because the old one broke.
cards.example_es: Compré una lavadora nueva porque la anterior se rompió.
cards.example_en: I bought a new washing machine because the old one broke.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a lava flow spilling into a giant washing machine, turning the lava into clean clothes.

cards.definition_en: A piece of absorbent fabric used for drying or wiping something.
cards.example_sentence: I dried my hands with a towel.
cards.example_es: Me sequé las manos con una toalla.
cards.example_en: I dried my hands with a towel.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a bright towel embroidered with TOALLA, soaking up water on the beach.

cards.definition_en: A cleansing substance, usually a solid or liquid, used for washing and removing dirt and oils.
cards.example_sentence: I bought soap to wash my hands.
cards.example_es: Compré jabón para lavar mis manos.
cards.example_en: I bought soap to wash my hands.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a soap bar shouting ja‑bone while scrubbing away dirt.

cards.definition_en: A liquid soap used for cleaning hair.
cards.example_sentence: I bought a shampoo that makes the hair very soft.
cards.example_es: Compré un champú que deja el cabello muy suave.
cards.example_en: I bought a shampoo that makes the hair very soft.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a sham poo being scrubbed with shampoo, linking Champú to the clean hair.

cards.definition_en: A tool with bristles used for cleaning, grooming, or applying substances.
cards.example_sentence: I need a brush to comb my hair.
cards.example_es: Necesito un cepillo para peinar mi cabello.
cards.example_en: I need a brush to comb my hair.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a soft pillow named sep that you use as a hair brush.

cards.definition_en: A place or system where goods and services are bought and sold.
cards.example_sentence: I go to the market every Saturday to buy fresh fruit.
cards.example_es: Voy al mercado cada sábado para comprar frutas frescas.
cards.example_en: I go to the market every Saturday to buy fresh fruit.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a bustling market where vendors shout 'Mercado' while selling goods.

cards.definition_en: A place where goods are sold to the public.
cards.example_sentence: I am going to the store to buy bread.
cards.example_es: Voy a la tienda a comprar pan.
cards.example_en: I am going to the store to buy bread.
cards.mnemonic_en: I store my snacks in a tiny tienda chest.

cards.definition_en: A large self-service store that sells a wide variety of food and household items.
cards.example_sentence: I go to the supermarket to buy fruit.
cards.example_es: Voy al supermercado para comprar frutas.
cards.example_en: I go to the supermarket to buy fruit.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a super market with a big sign reading Supermercado.

cards.definition_en: A written or printed list of items to be bought.
collocations: cross off items from the shopping list
cards.example_sentence: I'm going to prepare the shopping list before going to the supermarket.
cards.example_es: Voy a preparar la lista de compras antes de ir al supermercado.
cards.example_en: I'm going to prepare the shopping list before going to the supermarket.
cards.mnemonic_en: I see a grocery list, a 'lista' of shopping items pinned on the fridge.

cards.definition_en: A small wheeled vehicle used for carrying goods, often pulled by a person or animal.
cards.example_sentence: I bought a cart to carry my groceries to the market.
cards.example_es: Compré un carrito para llevar mis compras al mercado.
cards.example_en: I bought a cart to carry my groceries to the market.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a tiny cart rolling like a little car carrito in a grocery aisle.

cards.definition_en: A checkout counter is a place in a store where customers pay for their purchases.
cards.example_sentence: I'm going to the checkout counter to pay for my purchases.
cards.example_es: Voy a la caja para pagar mis compras.
cards.example_en: I'm going to the checkout counter to pay for my purchases.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cashier at the checkout counter shouting caja while scanning groceries

cards.definition_en: Money in the form of bills and coins that is used for transactions.
cards.example_sentence: I prefer to pay in cash.
cards.example_es: Prefiero pagar en efectivo.
cards.example_en: I prefer to pay in cash.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cash register flashing the word Efectivo on its screen.

cards.definition_en: A plastic or digital card issued by a financial institution that lets the holder borrow money to make purchases.
cards.example_sentence: I need to apply for a new credit card for my online purchases.
cards.example_es: Necesito solicitar una nueva tarjeta de crédito para mis compras en línea.
cards.example_en: I need to apply for a new credit card for my online purchases.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a credit card stamped with the word tarjeta, so credit equals tarjeta.

cards.definition_en: the act or result of making something different; a transformation or alteration
cards.example_sentence: I need a career change.
cards.example_es: Necesito un cambio de carrera.
cards.example_en: I need a career change.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cashier handing over a cambio of coins, instantly creating a fresh change.

cards.definition_en: A written acknowledgment that something has been received, often confirming a payment.
cards.example_sentence: The receipt from the purchase is on the table.
cards.example_es: El recibo de la compra está sobre la mesa.
cards.example_en: The receipt from the purchase is on the table.
cards.mnemonic_en: I keep a receipt (recibo) in my wallet as proof.

cards.definition_en: Low in price; inexpensive.
cards.example_sentence: This restaurant is very cheap.
cards.example_es: Este restaurante es muy barato.
cards.example_en: This restaurant is very cheap.
cards.mnemonic_en: A cheep bird perched on a cheap sign shouts Barato.

cards.definition_en: Costing a lot of money; having a high price.
cards.example_sentence: That car is very expensive.
cards.example_es: Ese coche es muy caro.
cards.example_en: That car is very expensive.
cards.mnemonic_en: A shiny car named Caro costs an expensive price.

cards.definition_en: A transaction in which goods or services are sold, often at a reduced price.
cards.example_sentence: Take advantage of the 50% off sale at the store.
cards.example_es: Aprovecha la oferta de 50% de descuento en la tienda.
cards.example_en: Take advantage of the 50% off sale at the store.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a SALE banner with a loud Oferta shouted by a street vendor.

cards.definition_en: Not closed or blocked, allowing access or passage.
cards.example_sentence: The restaurant is open.
cards.example_es: El restaurante está abierto.
cards.example_en: The restaurant is open.
cards.mnemonic_en: Open the door and hear a crowd shout abierto as the light spills in

cards.definition_en: Not open; shut.
cards.example_sentence: The restaurant is closed.
cards.example_es: El restaurante está cerrado.
cards.example_en: The restaurant is closed.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a closed door stamped with the word cerrado

cards.definition_en: A room or set of rooms where people work, especially in a business or organization.
cards.example_sentence: I work in an office downtown.
cards.example_es: Trabajo en una oficina en el centro de la ciudad.
cards.example_en: I work in an office downtown.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a busy office where every file is stamped with the word oficina.

cards.definition_en: A gathering of people for discussion or decision-making.
cards.example_sentence: The meeting will start at ten o'clock in the morning.
cards.example_es: La reunión comenzará a las diez de la mañana.
cards.example_en: The meeting will start at ten o'clock in the morning.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a meat‑ing where everyone reunites around a grill.

cards.definition_en: A person who works together with someone else in the same organization or on the same project.
cards.example_sentence: My coworker helped me finish the report.
cards.example_es: Mi compañero de trabajo me ayudó a terminar el informe.
cards.example_en: My coworker helped me finish the report.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cow at a desk working with your compañero, the friendly coworker.

cards.definition_en: A person who is in charge of others, especially at work.
cards.example_sentence: My boss gave me a raise last week.
cards.example_es: Mi jefe me dio un aumento la semana pasada.
cards.example_en: My boss gave me a raise last week.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine the jefe wearing a boss crown, shouting BOSS like a real boss.

cards.definition_en: A group of students who are taught together, or a category of objects or ideas that share common characteristics.
cards.example_sentence: The class is cancelled today.
cards.example_es: La clase está cancelada hoy.
cards.example_en: The class is cancelled today.
cards.mnemonic_en: I picture a teacher ringing a bell for each class, shouting Clase to start the lesson.

cards.definition_en: School assignments given to be completed outside of class.
cards.example_sentence: I have to finish my homework before dinner.
cards.example_es: Tengo que terminar mi tarea antes de la cena.
cards.example_en: I have to finish my homework before dinner.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a house (home) with a big 'tarea' sign on the door—homework.

cards.definition_en: To devote time and attention to acquiring knowledge or skill in a subject.
cards.example_sentence: I have to study for the exam.
cards.example_es: Tengo que estudiar para el examen.
cards.example_en: I have to study for the exam.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a stew bubbling in a pot labeled Estudiar as you study.

cards.definition_en: To acquire knowledge or skill through study, experience, or instruction.
cards.example_sentence: I want to learn to play the guitar.
cards.example_es: Quiero aprender a tocar la guitarra.
cards.example_en: I want to learn to play the guitar.
cards.mnemonic_en: I press the switch (prender) to learn the lesson.

cards.definition_en: A period of relaxation or inactivity intended to recover strength.
cards.example_sentence: I need to rest after work.
cards.example_es: Necesito descansar después del trabajo.
cards.example_en: I need to rest after work.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a weary traveler sitting on a rest bench called descANSAR for a quick rest

cards.definition_en: A plan that lists the times at which events or tasks are intended to occur.
cards.example_sentence: What is the meeting schedule?
cards.example_es: ¿Cuál es el horario de la reunión?
cards.example_en: What is the meeting schedule?
cards.mnemonic_en: I picture a school board with the word horario, and the schedule (sked-ule) pinned beside it.

cards.definition_en: The current day; the day that is happening now.
cards.example_sentence: Today I am going to the market.
cards.example_es: Hoy voy al mercado.
cards.example_en: Today I am going to the market.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a bright toy today, saying 'hoy' as you hold it.

cards.definition_en: The day after today.
cards.example_sentence: Tomorrow we will go to the park.
cards.example_es: Mañana iremos al parque.
cards.example_en: Tomorrow we will go to the park.
cards.mnemonic_en: Tomorrow I picture the sunrise and hear "mañana" whispering in my ear.

cards.definition_en: the day before today
cards.example_sentence: Yesterday we went to the cinema and watched an action movie.
cards.example_es: Ayer fuimos al cine y vimos una película de acción.
cards.example_en: Yesterday we went to the cinema and watched an action movie.
cards.mnemonic_en: Yesterday sounds like Spanish 'ayer', picture the day before today fading away.

cards.definition_en: the current week, referring to the seven-day period that includes today
cards.example_sentence: This week I go to the gym every day.
cards.example_es: Esta semana voy al gimnasio todos los días.
cards.example_en: This week I go to the gym every day.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a calendar saying esta week on the page linking this week to Spanish esta semana

cards.definition_en: The period of Saturday and Sunday, or the days at the end of the week, when most people are not working.
cards.example_sentence: This weekend I'm going to visit my grandparents.
cards.example_es: Este fin de semana voy a visitar a mis abuelos.
cards.example_en: This weekend I'm going to visit my grandparents.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture the week ending, a weekend, the fin of the week.

cards.definition_en: Moving or capable of moving at high speed.
cards.example_sentence: The car is fast.
cards.example_es: El coche es rápido.
cards.example_en: The car is fast.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a rabbit saying rapido as it darts fast.

cards.definition_en: In a slow manner; at a low speed.
cards.example_sentence: Walk slowly so you don't slip.
cards.example_es: Camina despacio para no resbalar.
cards.example_en: Walk slowly so you don't slip.
cards.mnemonic_en: A snail whispers despacio as it slides, reminding you everything moves slowly.

cards.definition_en: A polite request to pause briefly.
collocations: Wait a moment while I check
cards.example_sentence: Wait a moment while I look up the information.
cards.example_es: Espera un momento, voy a buscar la información.
cards.example_en: Wait a moment while I look up the information.
cards.mnemonic_en: I wait hearing the Spanish espera echo in the quiet hallway

cards.definition_en: A polite phrase used to get someone's attention or to apologize for a minor offense.
collocations: Excuse me, could you repeat that?
Excuse me, I have a question
Excuse me, may I sit here?
cards.example_sentence: Excuse me, can you pass me the salt?
cards.example_es: Perdón, ¿puedes pasarme la sal?
cards.example_en: Excuse me, can you pass me the salt?
cards.mnemonic_en: Excuse me, I'm a robot that says 'per‑done' when I apologize.

cards.definition_en: A polite response indicating that the speaker is happy to have helped.
cards.example_sentence: You're welcome, it was a pleasure to help you.
cards.example_es: De nada, fue un placer ayudarle.
cards.example_en: You're welcome, it was a pleasure to help you.
cards.mnemonic_en: Hear 'De nada' and picture a door opening, saying 'You are welcome' to the visitor.

cards.definition_en: A casual way to say goodbye, indicating that you will meet again later.
cards.example_sentence: See you later at the cafe.
cards.example_es: Nos vemos luego en el café.
cards.example_en: See you later at the cafe.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a friend waving and shouting nos vemos, then you both smile and think see you later

cards.definition_en: A polite phrase used to wish someone a pleasant night, typically said when parting in the evening.
cards.example_sentence: Good night, see you tomorrow.
cards.example_es: Buenas noches, nos vemos mañana.
cards.example_en: Good night, see you tomorrow.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a good night where 'buenas' sounds like good and 'noches' sounds like nights under a bright moon.

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that the speaker feels the need to eat.
cards.example_sentence: I'm hungry, so I'm going to make a sandwich.
cards.example_es: Tengo hambre, así que voy a preparar un sándwich.
cards.example_en: I'm hungry, so I'm going to make a sandwich.
cards.mnemonic_en: I am hungry, I picture a giant hamburger satisfying my hambre.

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that the speaker feels drowsy.
cards.example_sentence: I am sleepy after working all day.
cards.example_es: Tengo sueño después de trabajar todo el día.
cards.example_en: I am sleepy after working all day.
cards.mnemonic_en: I am sleepy like a tired llama whispering 'tengo sueño'.

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that the speaker feels cold.
collocations: I am cold, can you close the window?
I am cold and need a sweater
cards.example_sentence: I'm cold, so I'll put on a jacket.
cards.example_es: Tengo frío, así que me pondré una chaqueta.
cards.example_en: I'm cold, so I'll put on a jacket.
cards.mnemonic_en: I shiver, thinking 'tengo frío' like a frozen ego wearing a coat.

cards.definition_en: Used to say that you feel warm or have a high body temperature.
collocations: I am hot after the workout
cards.example_sentence: I am hot, so I'm going to open the window.
cards.example_es: Tengo calor, así que voy a abrir la ventana.
cards.example_en: I am hot, so I'm going to open the window.
cards.mnemonic_en: I am hot, like a blazing 'calor' flame scorching the desert.

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that the speaker feels fatigue.
cards.example_sentence: I am tired after running five kilometers.
cards.example_es: Estoy cansado después de correr cinco kilómetros.
cards.example_en: I am tired after running five kilometers.
cards.mnemonic_en: I am tired and open a sad empty can, feeling cansado

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that someone is occupied or has no free time.
cards.example_sentence: I am busy right now, but I will call you later.
cards.example_es: Estoy ocupado ahora, pero te llamaré más tarde.
cards.example_en: I am busy right now, but I will call you later.
cards.mnemonic_en: I picture a crowded desk, shouting Estoy ocupado as I juggle papers.

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that one is not occupied or constrained.
cards.example_sentence: I am free this weekend to go out.
cards.example_es: Estoy libre este fin de semana para salir.
cards.example_en: I am free this weekend to go out.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a free bird shouting Libre as it flies.

cards.definition_en: A command telling someone to remain in this place.
cards.example_sentence: Stay here while I prepare dinner.
cards.example_es: Quédate aquí mientras preparo la cena.
cards.example_en: Stay here while I prepare dinner.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a sign that says Stay here while hearing quédate aquí sounding like stay‑date.

cards.definition_en: A command telling someone to approach the speaker.
cards.example_sentence: When the dog ran away, the owner shouted, "Come here!"
cards.example_es: Cuando el perro se escapó, el dueño gritó: "¡Ven aquí!"
cards.example_en: When the dog ran away, the owner shouted, "Come here!"
cards.mnemonic_en: A dove says come here when you hear Ven aquí

cards.definition_en: To take a seat.
cards.example_sentence: Sit down on the chair and wait.
cards.example_es: Siéntate en la silla y espera.
cards.example_en: Sit down on the chair and wait.
cards.mnemonic_en: Sit down as the chair whispers siéntate, inviting you to rest.

cards.definition_en: To rise to a standing position.
cards.example_sentence: Stand up early so you don't arrive late to work.
cards.example_es: Levántate temprano para no llegar tarde al trabajo.
cards.example_en: Stand up early so you don't arrive late to work.
cards.mnemonic_en: Stand up as if pulling a lever that says levanta to lift yourself

cards.definition_en: A command telling someone to open the door.
cards.example_sentence: Open the door before the guests arrive.
cards.example_es: Abre la puerta antes de que lleguen los invitados.
cards.example_en: Open the door before the guests arrive.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a hand shouting abre as it opens the bright red door

cards.definition_en: A command telling someone to shut the window.
collocations: Make sure to close the window
Don't forget to close the window
cards.example_sentence: Close the window, please.
cards.example_es: Cierra la ventana, por favor.
cards.example_en: Close the window, please.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a saw (cierra) chopping the window closed.

cards.definition_en: To activate the light by switching it on.
cards.example_sentence: Turn on the light, please.
cards.example_es: Enciende la luz, por favor.
cards.example_en: Turn on the light, please.
cards.mnemonic_en: Flip the switch and shout 'luz' as the room brightens.

cards.definition_en: A command telling someone to switch the light off.
collocations: turn off the light before bed
cards.example_sentence: Please turn off the light when you leave the room.
cards.example_es: Por favor, apaga la luz cuando salgas de la habitación.
cards.example_en: Please turn off the light when you leave the room.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a torn-off lamp whispering apaga la luz as you turn off the light.

cards.definition_en: To clean the clothing by using water and detergent.
collocations: wash the clothes in cold water
cards.example_sentence: Wash the clothes before it starts to rain.
cards.example_es: Lava la ropa antes de que empiece a llover.
cards.example_en: Wash the clothes before it starts to rain.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture lava bubbling as it washes the clothes.

cards.definition_en: To remove the garbage from a location and place it in a waste container or outside for collection.
collocations: take out the trash before dinner
cards.example_sentence: Take out the trash before the guests arrive.
cards.example_es: Saca la basura antes de que lleguen los invitados.
cards.example_en: Take out the trash before the guests arrive.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a taco taking out trash from the bin

cards.definition_en: To purchase groceries or other needed items.
cards.example_sentence: Tomorrow, do the shopping for the food we need.
cards.example_es: Mañana haz la compra de los alimentos que necesitamos.
cards.example_en: Tomorrow, do the shopping for the food we need.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a hazy llama pushing a cart, doing the shopping.

cards.english: I am going to the bank
cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that the speaker is traveling to the bank.
collocations: I'm going to the bank tomorrow
I'm going to the bank later today
I'm going to the bank to deposit some money
I'm going to the bank for a loan
synonyms_en: on my way to the bank
cards.example_sentence: I'm going to the bank right now.
cards.example_es: Voy al banco ahora mismo.
cards.example_en: I'm going to the bank right now.
cards.mnemonic_en: I walk to the bank, picturing a wooden banco bench waiting for me.

cards.english: I am going to the pharmacy
cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that the speaker is heading to the pharmacy.
synonyms_en: I'm on my way to the pharmacy
cards.example_sentence: I am going to the pharmacy to buy medicine.
cards.example_es: Voy a la farmacia para comprar medicina.
cards.example_en: I am going to the pharmacy to buy medicine.
cards.mnemonic_en: I’m going to the pharmacy, a voy (voyage) to the farm of medicines.

cards.english: I am going to the gym
cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that the speaker is heading to the gym.
cards.example_sentence: I go to the gym every day to stay fit.
cards.example_es: Voy al gimnasio todos los días para mantenerme en forma.
cards.example_en: I go to the gym every day to stay fit.
cards.mnemonic_en: I picture myself walking into the gym, hearing the Spanish word 'gimnasio' echoing in the hallway.

cards.definition_en: To travel by riding a bus.
collocations: take the bus instead of driving
cards.example_sentence: Every morning, he takes the bus to go to work.
cards.example_es: Cada mañana, toma el autobús para ir al trabajo.
cards.example_en: Every morning, he takes the bus to go to work.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture Tom a bus driver waving, saying Take the bus.

cards.definition_en: A directive telling someone to stay until the train arrives.
collocations: wait for the train to arrive
wait for the train at the platform
wait for the train on time
cards.example_sentence: Wait for the train and then buy your ticket.
cards.example_es: Espera el tren y luego compra tu billete.
cards.example_en: Wait for the train and then buy your ticket.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture an esper wizard waiting for the train.

cards.definition_en: A command telling someone to telephone their mother.
main_translations_es: Haz una llamada a tu madre
collocations: Don't forget to call your mother
Make sure you call your mother
I need to call my mother
She told me to call your mother
cards.example_sentence: Call your mother when you get home.
cards.example_es: Llama a tu madre cuando llegues a casa.
cards.example_en: Call your mother when you get home.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine calling a llama and telling it call your mother

cards.definition_en: To transmit a written or electronic communication to someone.
cards.example_sentence: Send a message to your friend when you arrive.
cards.example_es: Envía un mensaje a tu amigo cuando llegues.
cards.example_en: Send a message to your friend when you arrive.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a courier handing over a note that says SEND like a Spanish envía sign

cards.definition_en: To look at or retrieve the email messages.
cards.example_sentence: Check the mail before you leave.
cards.example_es: Revisa el correo antes de salir.
cards.example_en: Check the mail before you leave.
cards.mnemonic_en: Check the mail, just like you 'chequeas' the inbox in Spanish.

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that the speaker requires rest.
collocations: I need to rest and recover
I need to rest for a while
I need to rest after work
cards.example_sentence: I need to rest after so much work.
cards.example_es: Necesito descansar después de tanto trabajo.
cards.example_en: I need to rest after so much work.
cards.mnemonic_en: I picture a tired llama needing a rest, whispering 'descansar' as it naps.

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that someone needs to act quickly because they have limited time.
cards.example_sentence: I'm in a hurry, so I can't stay long.
cards.example_es: Tengo prisa, así que no puedo quedarme mucho tiempo.
cards.example_en: I'm in a hurry, so I can't stay long.
cards.mnemonic_en: I am in a hurry, chasing a prize‑a rabbit.

cards.definition_en: A casual way of saying that something is not an issue or that you are happy to help.
cards.example_sentence: No problem, we can try again.
cards.example_es: No pasa nada, podemos intentarlo de nuevo.
cards.example_en: No problem, we can try again.
cards.mnemonic_en: No problem—no pasa nada, nothing passes by.

cards.definition_en: A person who lives near or next to another person.
cards.example_sentence: My neighbor helped me carry the boxes.
cards.example_es: Mi vecino me ayudó a cargar las cajas.
cards.example_en: My neighbor helped me carry the boxes.
cards.mnemonic_en: I see my neighbor waving a big ve sign, echoing the Spanish vecino.

cards.definition_en: A public area of land with grass, trees, and facilities for recreation and leisure.
cards.example_sentence: We go to the park to play soccer.
cards.example_es: Vamos al parque a jugar al fútbol.
cards.example_en: We go to the park to play soccer.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a park with a long queue of ducks waiting for bread.

title: English Basics
description: Everyday words and short expressions for beginners.

cards.definition_en: A word used to express farewell.
cards.example_sentence: Goodbye, see you tomorrow.
cards.example_es: Adiós, nos vemos mañana.
cards.example_en: Goodbye, see you tomorrow.
cards.mnemonic_en: I wave goodbye and shout adiós as the ship sails away.

cards.definition_en: A polite greeting used in the early afternoon.
cards.example_sentence: Good afternoon, how are you?
cards.example_es: Buenas tardes, ¿cómo está usted?
cards.example_en: Good afternoon, how are you?
cards.mnemonic_en: Think of a good (buenas) tardy (tardes) afternoon sipping coffee.

cards.definition_en: A polite greeting used in the early part of the night.
cards.example_sentence: Good evening, how was your day?
cards.example_es: Buenas noches, ¿cómo estuvo tu día?
cards.example_en: Good evening, how was your day?
cards.mnemonic_en: Think of a good night, because Good evening sounds like 'Buenas noches' under a bright moon.

cards.definition_en: A polite way to ask someone about their well-being.
cards.example_sentence: How are you?
cards.example_es: ¿Cómo estás?
cards.example_en: How are you?
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a coma patient asking How are you?

cards.definition_en: A polite response indicating that one is doing well.
cards.example_sentence: I'm fine, thanks for asking.
cards.example_es: Estoy bien, gracias por preguntar.
cards.example_en: I'm fine, thanks for asking.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a fine wine labeled bien, reminding you I am fine

cards.definition_en: An affirmative response used to indicate agreement or confirmation.
cards.example_sentence: Do you want to go to the movies? Yes, I would love to.
cards.example_es: ¿Quieres ir al cine? Sí, me encantaría.
cards.example_en: Do you want to go to the movies? Yes, I would love to.
cards.mnemonic_en: I nod and smile, shouting Yes like a bright sun saying sí.

cards.definition_en: A word used to give a negative answer or to refuse something.
cards.example_sentence: No, I don't have time.
cards.example_es: No, no tengo tiempo.
cards.example_en: No, I don't have time.

cards.definition_en: Perhaps; possibly.
cards.example_sentence: Maybe it will rain tomorrow.
cards.example_es: Tal vez llueva mañana.
cards.example_en: Maybe it will rain tomorrow.
cards.mnemonic_en: A tall (tal) friend shrugs, unsure, and says maybe.

cards.definition_en: the present day; the current date
cards.example_sentence: Today I'm going to the market.
cards.example_es: Hoy voy al mercado.
cards.example_en: Today I'm going to the market.
cards.mnemonic_en: A bright toy flashes the date, reminding you it's today.

cards.definition_en: The day after today.
cards.example_sentence: Tomorrow we will go to the park.
cards.example_es: Mañana iremos al parque.
cards.example_en: Tomorrow we will go to the park.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a tomato rowing a boat at sunrise, reminding you that mañana means tomorrow.

cards.definition_en: The day before today.
collocations: Yesterday, I went to the park
cards.example_sentence: Yesterday I went to the market and bought fruit.
cards.example_es: Ayer fui al mercado y compré frutas.
cards.example_en: Yesterday I went to the market and bought fruit.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a yawn (ayer) at sunrise, the day before today, that was yesterday.

cards.definition_en: Monday is the day of the week after Sunday and before Tuesday.
cards.example_sentence: On Monday I go to the market.
cards.example_es: El lunes voy al mercado.
cards.example_en: On Monday I go to the market.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a bright moon on Monday lighting the word lunes on a calendar.

cards.definition_en: Tuesday is the second day of the week, following Monday and preceding Wednesday.
synonyms_en: the second day of the week
cards.example_sentence: Next Tuesday we will go to the movies.
cards.example_es: El próximo martes iremos al cine.
cards.example_en: Next Tuesday we will go to the movies.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a tiger dancing on Tuesday, yelling Martes.

cards.definition_en: Wednesday is the third day of the week, following Tuesday and preceding Thursday.
cards.example_sentence: Next Wednesday we will go to the movies.
cards.example_es: El próximo miércoles iremos al cine.
cards.example_en: Next Wednesday we will go to the movies.
cards.mnemonic_en: Wednesday sounds like when's day, imagine a mid‑week market called miércoles.
