cards.definition_en: Thursday is the fourth day of the week, following Wednesday and preceding Friday.
cards.example_sentence: Next Thursday we will go to the movies.
cards.example_es: El próximo jueves iremos al cine.
cards.example_en: Next Thursday we will go to the movies.
cards.mnemonic_en: Throw a juicy orange on Thursday to remember Jueves.

cards.definition_en: Friday is the fifth day of the week, following Thursday and preceding Saturday.
cards.example_sentence: On Friday we will go to the movies.
cards.example_es: El viernes iremos al cine.
cards.example_en: On Friday we will go to the movies.
cards.mnemonic_en: I celebrate Friday by raising a 'vier' (beer) toast, linking to Viernes.

cards.definition_en: Saturday is the seventh day of the week, following Friday and preceding Sunday.
cards.example_sentence: Next Saturday we will go to the park.
cards.example_es: El próximo sábado iremos al parque.
cards.example_en: Next Saturday we will go to the park.
cards.mnemonic_en: On Saturday I hear friends cheer Sábado as we start the weekend.

cards.definition_en: Sunday is the day of the week that follows Saturday and precedes Monday.
cards.example_sentence: Next Sunday we will go to the park.
cards.example_es: El próximo domingo iremos al parque.
cards.example_en: Next Sunday we will go to the park.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a bright Sunday with a golden dome rising over the town.

cards.definition_en: January is the first month of the year in the Gregorian calendar.
cards.example_sentence: My birthday is in January.
cards.example_es: Mi cumpleaños es en enero.
cards.example_en: My birthday is in January.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a January snowflake shaped like the letter E for Enero.

cards.definition_en: The second month of the year in the Gregorian calendar.
cards.example_sentence: My birthday is in February.
cards.example_es: Mi cumpleaños es en febrero.
cards.example_en: My birthday is in February.
cards.mnemonic_en: A feverish bear (Febrero) shivers during February.

cards.definition_en: The third month of the year in the Gregorian calendar.
cards.example_sentence: My birthday is in March.
cards.example_es: Mi cumpleaños es en marzo.
cards.example_en: My birthday is in March.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a marching band in March holding a banner that reads 'Marzo'.

cards.definition_en: the fourth month of the year in the Gregorian calendar
cards.example_sentence: My birthday is in April.
cards.example_es: Mi cumpleaños es en abril.
cards.example_en: My birthday is in April.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a bright spring flower blooming in April, echoing the Spanish word Abril

cards.definition_en: the fifth month of the year in the Gregorian calendar
collocations: May the force be with you
cards.example_sentence: My birthday is in May.
cards.example_es: Mi cumpleaños es en mayo.
cards.example_en: My birthday is in May.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a mayo jar dancing in May

cards.definition_en: June is the sixth month of the year in the Gregorian calendar.
cards.example_sentence: My birthday is in June.
cards.example_es: Mi cumpleaños es en junio.
cards.example_en: My birthday is in June.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a sunny June garden where a rooster says junio to welcome the month

cards.definition_en: July is the seventh month of the year in the Gregorian calendar.
cards.example_sentence: My birthday is in July.
cards.example_es: Mi cumpleaños es en julio.
cards.example_en: My birthday is in July.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture Julio lighting fireworks for the Fourth of July celebration.

cards.definition_en: The eighth month of the year in the Gregorian calendar.
cards.example_sentence: My birthday is in August.
cards.example_es: Mi cumpleaños es en agosto.
cards.example_en: My birthday is in August.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a gust of wind blowing over the Spanish calendar on Agosto, the month of August.

cards.definition_en: September is the ninth month of the year in the Gregorian calendar.
cards.example_sentence: My birthday is in September.
cards.example_es: Mi cumpleaños es en septiembre.
cards.example_en: My birthday is in September.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a septum (sept) on a calendar, showing the month September.

cards.definition_en: October is the tenth month of the year in the Gregorian calendar.
cards.example_sentence: My birthday is in October.
cards.example_es: Mi cumpleaños es en octubre.
cards.example_en: My birthday is in October.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture an octopus (oct) with a blue leaf in October, chanting Octubre.

cards.definition_en: The eleventh month of the year in the Gregorian calendar.
cards.example_sentence: My birthday is in November.
cards.example_es: Mi cumpleaños es en noviembre.
cards.example_en: My birthday is in November.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a new (nuevo) calendar page turning to November hearing Noviembre echo like a fresh start

cards.definition_en: December is the twelfth month of the year in the Gregorian calendar.
synonyms_en: the final month of the year
cards.example_sentence: My birthday is in December.
cards.example_es: Mi cumpleaños es en diciembre.
cards.example_en: My birthday is in December.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a dice falling into December because Diciembre sounds like dice.

cards.definition_en: The number that follows zero and precedes two.
cards.example_sentence: One of my favorite books is "One Hundred Years of Solitude".
cards.example_es: Uno de mis libros favoritos es "Cien años de soledad".
cards.example_en: One of my favorite books is "One Hundred Years of Solitude".
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a single UNO card labeled ONE on the table.

cards.definition_en: The cardinal number that follows one and precedes three.
cards.example_sentence: I have two dogs.
cards.example_es: Tengo dos perros.
cards.example_en: I have two dogs.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture two dice forming the letters D‑O‑S on the table.

cards.definition_en: The cardinal number that follows two and precedes four.
cards.example_sentence: I have three books.
cards.example_es: Tengo tres libros.
cards.example_en: I have three books.
cards.mnemonic_en: Three tall trees sway, reminding me of tres.

cards.definition_en: The number that comes after three and before five.
cards.example_sentence: I have four books on the table.
cards.example_es: Tengo cuatro libros en la mesa.
cards.example_en: I have four books on the table.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a four‑leaf clover shaped like a tiny Cuatro guitar.

cards.definition_en: The cardinal number that comes after four and before six.
cards.example_sentence: I have five apples.
cards.example_es: Tengo cinco manzanas.
cards.example_en: I have five apples.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize five fingers sinking into a jar labeled CINCO.

cards.definition_en: The integer that comes after five and before seven.
collocations: six of one, half a dozen
cards.example_sentence: Six people entered the cinema.
cards.example_es: Seis personas entraron al cine.
cards.example_en: Six people entered the cinema.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine six dice shouting seis as they tumble across the table.

cards.definition_en: The cardinal number that comes after six and before eight.
cards.example_sentence: I have seven books on my shelf.
cards.example_es: Tengo siete libros en mi estantería.
cards.example_en: I have seven books on my shelf.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a lucky seven dice shouting siete as it rolls.

cards.definition_en: The number that comes after seven and before nine.
cards.example_sentence: I have eight apples.
cards.example_es: Tengo ocho manzanas.
cards.example_en: I have eight apples.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture an octopus juggling eight shells while saying ocho.

cards.definition_en: The number that comes after eight and before ten.
cards.example_sentence: I have nine books on my shelf.
cards.example_es: Tengo nueve libros en mi estantería.
cards.example_en: I have nine books on my shelf.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine nine neon signs spelling N-I-N-E above a Spanish fiesta where everyone shouts Nueve.

cards.definition_en: The number that follows nine and precedes eleven.
cards.example_sentence: I have ten books on my shelf.
cards.example_es: Tengo diez libros en mi estantería.
cards.example_en: I have ten books on my shelf.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture ten dice tumbling, because diez sounds like dice.

cards.definition_en: The color at the end of the visible spectrum next to orange and opposite violet.
cards.example_sentence: The red car is parked in front of the house.
cards.example_es: El coche rojo está estacionado frente a la casa.
cards.example_en: The red car is parked in front of the house.
cards.mnemonic_en: Red like a ripe rojo tomato.

cards.definition_en: A color that resembles the clear sky on a sunny day.
cards.example_sentence: The sky is blue today.
cards.example_es: El cielo está azul hoy.
cards.example_en: The sky is blue today.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a blue zoo—azul—where every animal is painted sky‑blue.

cards.definition_en: A color between blue and yellow in the visible spectrum.
cards.example_sentence: The car is green.
cards.example_es: El coche es verde.
cards.example_en: The car is green.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a green frog shouting Verde!

cards.definition_en: A bright color between green and orange in the visible spectrum.
cards.example_sentence: The car I bought is yellow.
cards.example_es: El coche que compré es de color amarillo.
cards.example_en: The car I bought is yellow.
cards.mnemonic_en: A bright yellow sun shouts amarillo across the sky

cards.definition_en: The darkest color, resulting from the absence or complete absorption of visible light.
cards.example_sentence: The black cat sleeps on the sofa.
cards.example_es: El gato negro duerme en el sofá.
cards.example_en: The black cat sleeps on the sofa.
cards.mnemonic_en: The black coat matches the dark negro night sky

cards.definition_en: The color of milk or snow, a light, achromatic hue.
cards.example_sentence: The paper is white and ready to write.
cards.example_es: El papel es blanco y listo para escribir.
cards.example_en: The paper is white and ready to write.
cards.mnemonic_en: White like the blanco snow sparkling under the sun

cards.definition_en: A round citrus fruit with a thick orange skin and sweet, juicy flesh.
cards.example_sentence: I bought an orange for breakfast.
cards.example_es: Compré una naranja para el desayuno.
cards.example_en: I bought an orange for breakfast.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a bright orange fruit shouting Naranja at the market.

cards.definition_en: A pale red color.
cards.example_sentence: I like the pink shirt.
cards.example_es: Me gusta la camisa rosa.
cards.example_en: I like the pink shirt.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a pink rose, a bright rosa, blooming to remind you pink.

cards.definition_en: A dark orange‑red color resembling chocolate or earth.
cards.example_sentence: I bought a brown jacket for the fall.
cards.example_es: Compré una chaqueta marrón para el otoño.
cards.example_en: I bought a brown jacket for the fall.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a brown bear wearing a maroon (marrón) scarf.

cards.definition_en: A neutral color that lies between black and white.
cards.example_sentence: The sky is gray today.
cards.example_es: El cielo está gris hoy.
cards.example_en: The sky is gray today.
cards.mnemonic_en: A gray stone rolls and mutters 'gris' to the wind.

cards.definition_en: A female parent.
cards.example_sentence: My mother cooks dinner every night.
cards.example_es: Mi madre cocina la cena todas las noches.
cards.example_en: My mother cooks dinner every night.
cards.mnemonic_en: A mother cradles a mad rabbit named Madre, linking mother and madre.

cards.definition_en: A male parent.
cards.example_sentence: My father taught me how to ride a bike.
cards.example_es: Mi padre me enseñó a montar en bicicleta.
cards.example_en: My father taught me how to ride a bike.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a dad holding a pad, shouting 'pa!'—that's a Father.

cards.definition_en: A male sibling.
cards.example_sentence: My brother lives in Madrid.
cards.example_es: Mi hermano vive en Madrid.
cards.example_en: My brother lives in Madrid.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture your brother shaking your hand, because brother sounds like her mano.

cards.definition_en: A female sibling.
cards.example_sentence: My sister lives in Madrid.
cards.example_es: Mi hermana vive en Madrid.
cards.example_en: My sister lives in Madrid.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture your sister giving a man a bottle of her mana.

cards.definition_en: A male child or offspring.
cards.example_sentence: My son is studying medicine at university.
cards.example_es: Mi hijo está estudiando medicina en la universidad.
cards.example_en: My son is studying medicine at university.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a son yelling 'hee-yo!' in a sunny field, linking Son to Spanish hijo.

cards.definition_en: A female child in relation to her parents.
cards.example_sentence: My daughter is studying medicine at the university.
cards.example_es: Mi hija está estudiando medicina en la universidad.
cards.example_en: My daughter is studying medicine at the university.
cards.mnemonic_en: A daughter cheers hee ya while climbing the tree.

cards.definition_en: A person with whom one shares a bond of mutual affection and trust.
cards.example_sentence: My friend helped me move on Saturday.
cards.example_es: Mi amigo me ayudó a mudarme el sábado.
cards.example_en: My friend helped me move on Saturday.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine an AMIGO smiling as he greets his FRIEND with a warm hug

cards.definition_en: A group of related individuals, such as parents and their children, or people connected by blood, marriage, or adoption.
cards.example_sentence: My family gets together every Sunday for dinner.
cards.example_es: Mi familia se reúne cada domingo para cenar juntos.
cards.example_en: My family gets together every Sunday for dinner.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a happy family gathered around a table with a big banner reading familia

cards.definition_en: A very young child or infant.
cards.example_sentence: The baby is sleeping in its crib.
cards.example_es: El bebé está durmiendo en su cuna.
cards.example_en: The baby is sleeping in its crib.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a tiny baby cooing "bebe" like a soft lullaby.

cards.definition_en: A young human being.
cards.example_sentence: The child plays in the park.
cards.example_es: El niño juega en el parque.
cards.example_en: The child plays in the park.
cards.mnemonic_en: A child kneels and says "knee, no!"

cards.definition_en: the upper part of the human body that contains the brain, eyes, ears, nose, and mouth
cards.example_sentence: My head hurts.
cards.example_es: Me duele la cabeza.
cards.example_en: My head hurts.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a hedgehog balancing a tiny hat on its cabeza.

cards.definition_en: The organ of sight in humans and many animals.
collocations: in the blink of an eye
cards.example_sentence: My eye hurts.
cards.example_es: Me duele el ojo.
cards.example_en: My eye hurts.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture an eye shaped like an O that says 'jo' when you look.

cards.definition_en: The protruding organ on the face used for smelling and breathing.
cards.example_sentence: My nose hurts when I breathe cold air.
cards.example_es: Me duele la nariz cuando respiro aire frío.
cards.example_en: My nose hurts when I breathe cold air.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a nose shouting no while a giant naríz watches it.

cards.definition_en: The opening in the face through which a person eats, speaks, and breathes.
cards.example_sentence: My mouth hurts after eating ice cream.
cards.example_es: Me duele la boca después de comer helado.
cards.example_en: My mouth hurts after eating ice cream.
cards.mnemonic_en: My mouth says boca when I chew tacos

cards.definition_en: The five‑fingered, prehensile extremity at the end of a human arm used for grasping and manipulating objects.
cards.example_sentence: My hand hurts after hitting the table.
cards.example_es: Me duele la mano después de golpear la mesa.
cards.example_en: My hand hurts after hitting the table.
cards.mnemonic_en: I hand you a mano of cards.

cards.definition_en: The upper limb of the human body extending from the shoulder to the hand.
cards.example_sentence: My arm hurts after lifting weights.
cards.example_es: Me duele el brazo después de levantar pesas.
cards.example_en: My arm hurts after lifting weights.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a muscular arm holding a bra, turning it into a brazo.

cards.definition_en: A limb of the human body used for standing and walking, extending from the hip to the foot.
cards.example_sentence: My leg hurts after running.
cards.example_es: Me duele la pierna después de correr.
cards.example_en: My leg hurts after running.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a leg leaning on a pier, the pier says na to keep it steady.

cards.definition_en: The lower extremity of the leg on which a person stands and walks.
cards.example_sentence: My foot hurts after running.
cards.example_es: Me duele el pie después de correr.
cards.example_en: My foot hurts after running.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a foot stepping on a tasty pie, because foot sounds just like the Spanish word pie.

cards.definition_en: The rear surface of a person or object, opposite the front.
cards.example_sentence: My back hurts after running.
cards.example_es: Me duele la espalda después de correr.
cards.example_en: My back hurts after running.
cards.mnemonic_en: I turned my back and felt my espalda ache.

cards.definition_en: The front part of a person's head that includes the eyes, nose, and mouth.
cards.example_sentence: She has a very friendly face.
cards.example_es: Ella tiene una cara muy amable.
cards.example_en: She has a very friendly face.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a bright cara painted on every face you meet.

cards.definition_en: Hair is the thin strands that grow from the skin of humans and animals, especially on the head.
cards.example_sentence: I cut my hair yesterday.
cards.example_es: Me corté el pelo ayer.
cards.example_en: I cut my hair yesterday.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a single hair as a tiny 'pelo' dancing on a breeze.

cards.definition_en: The organ of hearing located on each side of the head.
collocations: in one ear and out the other
cards.example_sentence: My right ear hurts.
cards.example_es: Me duele la oreja derecha.
cards.example_en: My right ear hurts.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture an ear shaped like an OREJA, the Spanish word, catching sound waves.

cards.definition_en: A clear, colorless, odorless, and tasteless liquid that makes up seas, lakes, rivers, and rain, and is essential for most life.
cards.example_sentence: I need to drink water.
cards.example_es: Necesito beber agua.
cards.example_en: I need to drink water.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a blue aqua (agua) faucet gushing water.

cards.definition_en: A staple food made from flour and water, typically baked.
cards.example_sentence: I like to eat fresh bread every morning.
cards.example_es: Me gusta comer pan recién horneado cada mañana.
cards.example_en: I like to eat fresh bread every morning.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a pan of dough turning into fresh bread.

cards.definition_en: Milk is the white liquid produced by the mammary glands of mammals, commonly consumed as a beverage and used in cooking.
cards.example_sentence: I like to drink milk before going to bed.
cards.example_es: Me gusta tomar leche antes de dormir.
cards.example_en: I like to drink milk before going to bed.
cards.mnemonic_en: Milk sounds like mil—imagine a cow saying leche as it gives you fresh milk.

cards.definition_en: A round fruit with red, green, or yellow skin and a sweet, crisp flesh, typically grown on apple trees.
cards.example_sentence: I bought an apple at the market.
cards.example_es: Compré una manzana en el mercado.
cards.example_en: I bought an apple at the market.
cards.mnemonic_en: A man bites a crisp apple and shouts zana!

cards.definition_en: A domesticated carnivorous mammal commonly kept as a pet or used for work, known for its loyalty and barking.
cards.example_sentence: The dog runs in the park.
cards.example_es: El perro corre en el parque.
cards.example_en: The dog runs in the park.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a dog wearing a pair of O-shaped medals around its neck.

cards.definition_en: A small domesticated carnivorous mammal with soft fur, often kept as a pet.
cards.example_sentence: The cat sleeps on the sofa.
cards.example_es: El gato duerme en el sofá.
cards.example_en: The cat sleeps on the sofa.
cards.mnemonic_en: A cat named Gato purrs while chasing a bright laser dot

cards.definition_en: A bound collection of written or printed pages that can be read.
cards.example_sentence: I am reading an interesting book.
cards.example_es: Estoy leyendo un libro interesante.
cards.example_en: I am reading an interesting book.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a librarian shouting 'Libro!' as he hands you a huge book.

cards.definition_en: An institution where children and young adults receive education.
cards.example_sentence: The school is closed today.
cards.example_es: La escuela está cerrada hoy.
cards.example_en: The school is closed today.
cards.mnemonic_en: School sounds like es-cool-a, so picture a super cool school building.

cards.definition_en: A person who teaches, especially in a school.
cards.example_sentence: The teacher explained the lesson clearly.
cards.example_es: El profesor explicó la lección con claridad.
cards.example_en: The teacher explained the lesson clearly.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a teacher in a professor's cap shouting teach er like a Spanish profesor.

cards.definition_en: A person who is enrolled in an educational institution or is studying.
cards.example_sentence: The student studies for the exam.
cards.example_es: El estudiante estudia para el examen.
cards.example_en: The student studies for the exam.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a stud wearing an est badge, reminding you that student means estudiante.

cards.definition_en: To cause something to become not closed or to allow access.
cards.example_sentence: I am going to open the window.
cards.example_es: Voy a abrir la ventana.
cards.example_en: I am going to open the window.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a breezy wind abrir the door and the door swings open

cards.definition_en: To shut something so that it is no longer open.
cards.example_sentence: Please close the door when you leave.
cards.example_es: Por favor, cierra la puerta cuando salgas.
cards.example_en: Please close the door when you leave.
cards.mnemonic_en: I close the lid and hear the Spanish word cerrar humming.

cards.definition_en: To look at and understand the meaning of written or printed material.
cards.example_sentence: I like to read before going to bed.
cards.example_es: Me gusta leer antes de dormir.
cards.example_en: I like to read before going to bed.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a red book titled Leer on the shelf as you read.

cards.definition_en: To produce text or symbols on a surface using a pen, pencil, keyboard, or other instrument.
cards.example_sentence: I like to write letters by hand.
cards.example_es: Me gusta escribir cartas a mano.
cards.example_en: I like to write letters by hand.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a pen writing 'es' on a crib, reminding you that escribir means write.

cards.definition_en: To give attention to sounds by hearing them.
cards.example_sentence: I like to listen to music while I study.
cards.example_es: Me gusta escuchar música mientras estudio.
cards.example_en: I like to listen to music while I study.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a pirate whispering "escucha" as he leans in, urging you to listen.

cards.definition_en: To utter words or communicate verbally.
cards.example_sentence: I like to speak with my friends.
cards.example_es: Me gusta hablar con mis amigos.
cards.example_en: I like to speak with my friends.
cards.mnemonic_en: A tiny speck whispers 'habla' into your ear, reminding you that speak means hablar.

cards.definition_en: To consume food or drink.
cards.example_sentence: I like to eat pizza on Fridays.
cards.example_es: Me gusta comer pizza los viernes.
cards.example_en: I like to eat pizza on Fridays.
cards.mnemonic_en: When I eat, a loud COMER echo rings from my stomach.

cards.definition_en: To consume a liquid.
cards.example_sentence: I like to drink water after running.
cards.example_es: Me gusta beber agua después de correr.
cards.example_en: I like to drink water after running.
cards.mnemonic_en: I sip a cold 'beber' brew every time I drink.

cards.definition_en: to move or travel from one place to another
cards.example_sentence: I go to the market every morning.
cards.example_es: Voy al mercado cada mañana.
cards.example_en: I go to the market every morning.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a goat shouting go as it runs, hearing the Spanish Ir echo across the hills.

cards.definition_en: to move or travel toward a place or person
cards.example_sentence: Can you come to my house tomorrow?
cards.example_es: ¿Puedes venir a mi casa mañana?
cards.example_en: Can you come to my house tomorrow?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a dog barking Come while you venir from the garden

cards.definition_en: To perceive something with the eyes.
cards.example_sentence: Can you see the mountain from here?
cards.example_es: ¿Puedes ver la montaña desde aquí?
cards.example_en: Can you see the mountain from here?
cards.mnemonic_en: I see a ver-y clear sky.

cards.definition_en: To desire or wish for something.
cards.example_sentence: I want to travel to Spain this summer.
cards.example_es: Quiero viajar a España este verano.
cards.example_en: I want to travel to Spain this summer.
cards.mnemonic_en: I want a wan (pale) taco because I 'querer' it.

cards.definition_en: To require something because it is necessary or desirable.
cards.example_sentence: I need to buy milk.
cards.example_es: Necesito comprar leche.
cards.example_en: I need to buy milk.
cards.mnemonic_en: My knee needs a seat on the bench.

cards.definition_en: Having a large size, extent, or magnitude.
cards.example_sentence: The dog is very big.
cards.example_es: El perro es muy grande.
cards.example_en: The dog is very big.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a BIG elephant wearing a GRANDE sombrero

cards.definition_en: Having a limited size, amount, or extent.
cards.example_sentence: The dog is small but very brave.
cards.example_es: El perro es pequeño pero muy valiente.
cards.example_en: The dog is small but very brave.
cards.mnemonic_en: A tiny (pequeño) mouse sniffs a small crumb.

cards.definition_en: Recently created, discovered, or introduced; not existing before.
cards.example_sentence: I bought a new car.
cards.example_es: Compré un coche nuevo.
cards.example_en: I bought a new car.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a newborn baby holding a fresh, new blanket, echoing the nu sound of nuevo

cards.definition_en: Having existed or been in use for a long time; not new.
cards.example_sentence: The old book is on the shelf.
cards.example_es: El libro viejo está en la estantería.
cards.example_en: The old book is on the shelf.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture an old wizard saying VIEJO while opening a dusty ancient book.

cards.definition_en: Feeling or showing pleasure, contentment, or joy.
cards.example_sentence: I am happy with the results.
cards.example_es: Estoy feliz con los resultados.
cards.example_en: I am happy with the results.
cards.mnemonic_en: A happy clown shouts Feliz as he receives a big birthday cake.

title: Cafe Counter Basics
description: Short English needed for simple cafe orders.

cards.definition_en: A clear, colorless, odorless, and tasteless liquid that makes up seas, lakes, rivers, and rain, and is essential for most life.
cards.example_sentence: I need to drink water after running.
cards.example_es: Necesito beber agua después de correr.
cards.example_en: I need to drink water after running.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a clear water splash shouting agua as it hits the shore

cards.definition_en: A carbonated water beverage, often served as a non‑alcoholic drink.
cards.example_sentence: I would like to order sparkling water, please.
cards.example_es: Me gustaría pedir agua con gas, por favor.
cards.example_en: I would like to order sparkling water, please.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture sparkling water bubbling like gas in a glass of agua.

cards.definition_en: A liquid extracted from fruits or vegetables, typically consumed as a drink.
cards.example_sentence: I like to drink orange juice at breakfast.
cards.example_es: Me gusta tomar zumo de naranja en el desayuno.
cards.example_en: I like to drink orange juice at breakfast.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a zoo cow sipping bright orange juice, the word zumo echoing in your mind.

cards.definition_en: A beverage made by extracting the liquid from oranges.
cards.example_sentence: I like to drink a glass of orange juice every morning.
cards.example_es: Me gusta tomar un vaso de zumo de naranja cada mañana.
cards.example_en: I like to drink a glass of orange juice every morning.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a bright orange spilling its zumo into a glass of juice.

cards.definition_en: A warm beverage made from melted chocolate or cocoa powder, milk, and sugar.
cards.example_sentence: I like drinking hot chocolate on cold mornings.
cards.example_es: Me gusta beber chocolate caliente en las mañanas frías.
cards.example_en: I like drinking hot chocolate on cold mornings.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a steaming mug of hot chocolate and hear caliente sizzling as the heat rises.

cards.definition_en: A white liquid produced by the mammary glands of mammals, commonly consumed as a beverage.
cards.example_sentence: I like drinking milk with cereal.
cards.example_es: Me gusta tomar leche con cereal.
cards.example_en: I like drinking milk with cereal.
cards.mnemonic_en: See a sleek milk bottle stamped with the word Leche, so the English milk instantly triggers the Spanish label.

cards.definition_en: A plant-based milk made by soaking and blending oats with water.
cards.example_sentence: I like to have oat milk for breakfast.
cards.example_es: Me gusta tomar una bebida de avena en el desayuno.
cards.example_en: I like to have oat milk for breakfast.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cow sipping oat milk while chanting avena like a chant.

cards.definition_en: A plant-based beverage made from soybeans.
cards.example_sentence: I like soy milk at breakfast.
cards.example_es: Me gusta la bebida de soja en el desayuno.
cards.example_en: I like soy milk at breakfast.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a soy bean shouting soy as it turns into a white milk splash.

cards.definition_en: A non-alcoholic, carbonated beverage.
cards.example_sentence: I bought a cold soft drink to go with the pizza.
cards.example_es: Me compré un refresco frío para acompañar la pizza.
cards.example_en: I bought a cold soft drink to go with the pizza.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cold soft drink that refreshes you like a gust called Refresco on a hot day.

cards.definition_en: A sweetened drink made from lemon juice, water, and sugar.
cards.example_sentence: I like drinking fresh lemonade on hot days.
cards.example_es: Me gusta tomar limonada fresca en los días calurosos.
cards.example_en: I like drinking fresh lemonade on hot days.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a bright lemon drink, because lemonade sounds like limon in limonada.

cards.definition_en: A cold, sweet beverage made by blending milk with ice cream or flavorings.
cards.example_sentence: I want a strawberry milkshake.
cards.example_es: Quiero un batido de fresa.
cards.example_en: I want a strawberry milkshake.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a bat shaking a milk bottle, turning it into a milkshake

cards.definition_en: Coffee that has had most of its caffeine removed.
cards.example_sentence: I like to drink decaf coffee at night.
cards.example_es: Me gusta tomar café descafeinado por la noche.
cards.example_en: I like to drink decaf coffee at night.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a coffee saying de‑caf to the café, showing it’s caffeine‑free.

cards.definition_en: A strong, concentrated coffee made by forcing hot water through finely ground coffee beans.
cards.example_sentence: I like to have an espresso in the morning.
cards.example_es: Me gusta tomar un espresso por la mañana.
cards.example_en: I like to have an espresso in the morning.

cards.definition_en: A coffee beverage made with espresso and steamed milk, topped with a layer of foamed milk.
cards.example_sentence: I would like to order a cappuccino.
cards.example_es: Me gustaría pedir un capuchino.
cards.example_en: I would like to order a cappuccino.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a capuchin monkey sipping a frothy cappuccino.

cards.definition_en: A coffee beverage made with espresso and steamed milk.
cards.example_sentence: I would like a latte, please.
cards.example_es: Quiero un café con leche, por favor.
cards.example_en: I would like a latte, please.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a latte, a coffee with milk, as the Spanish café con leche in a creamy cup.

cards.definition_en: A cold beverage made by chilling tea, often served with ice.
collocations: iced tea on a hot day
cards.example_sentence: I like drinking iced tea on hot days.
cards.example_es: Me gusta tomar té helado en los días calurosos.
cards.example_en: I like drinking iced tea on hot days.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a frosty cup of iced tea spilling over a snow‑capped 'té' hill.

cards.definition_en: A buttery, flaky, crescent-shaped pastry commonly eaten for breakfast.
cards.example_sentence: I love eating a freshly baked croissant in the morning.
cards.example_es: Me encanta comer un croissant recién horneado en la mañana.
cards.example_en: I love eating a freshly baked croissant in the morning.

cards.definition_en: A small, sweet, individual cake or quick bread, typically round and baked in a cup-shaped mold.
cards.example_sentence: I ate a muffin for breakfast.
cards.example_es: Me comí una magdalena en el desayuno.
cards.example_en: I ate a muffin for breakfast.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture Magdalena the saint handing you a warm golden muffin.

cards.definition_en: A sweet baked snack, typically small, flat, and either crisp or soft.
cards.example_sentence: I ate a chocolate cookie after dinner.
cards.example_es: Me comí una galleta de chocolate después de la cena.
cards.example_en: I ate a chocolate cookie after dinner.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cook placing a golden galleta on a plate, calling it a cookie.

cards.definition_en: A sweet baked dessert typically made from flour, sugar, and other ingredients.
collocations: have your cake and eat it too
cards.example_sentence: I bought a chocolate cake for my sister's birthday.
cards.example_es: Compré una tarta de chocolate para el cumpleaños de mi hermana.
cards.example_en: I bought a chocolate cake for my sister's birthday.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cake wearing a tarta flag, because cake sounds like 'c-a-ke' and tarta means tart.

cards.definition_en: A food item consisting of two slices of bread with a filling such as meat, cheese, or vegetables placed between them.
cards.example_sentence: I ate a ham and cheese sandwich in the park.
cards.example_es: Me comí un bocadillo de jamón y queso en el parque.
cards.example_en: I ate a ham and cheese sandwich in the park.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a sandwich on a beach of sand, like a bocadillo floating in the sea.

cards.definition_en: A slice of bread that has been browned by heat.
cards.example_sentence: I like to eat toast with butter and jam.
cards.example_es: Me gusta comer tostada con mantequilla y mermelada.
cards.example_en: I like to eat toast with butter and jam.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a crunchy toast popping out of a huge Tostada sandwich.

cards.definition_en: A dish made of mixed raw or cooked vegetables, often served with a dressing.
cards.example_sentence: I like to eat salad with chicken.
cards.example_es: Me gusta comer ensalada con pollo.
cards.example_en: I like to eat salad with chicken.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a bowl of lettuce chanting 'ensalada' while you mix the fresh salad.

cards.definition_en: A liquid dish made by boiling ingredients such as meat, vegetables, or legumes in water or stock.
cards.example_sentence: I like hot soup in winter.
cards.example_es: Me gusta la sopa caliente en invierno.
cards.example_en: I like hot soup in winter.
cards.mnemonic_en: Soap bubbles pop on a steaming bowl of soup.

cards.definition_en: A creamy, fermented dairy product made from milk, commonly eaten as a snack or used in cooking.
cards.example_sentence: I like to eat yogurt with fruit for breakfast.
cards.example_es: Me gusta comer yogur con frutas en el desayuno.
cards.example_en: I like to eat yogurt with fruit for breakfast.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a yogurt cup wearing a tiny yogur label like a flag.

cards.definition_en: A dairy product made from curdled milk, commonly eaten as food.
cards.example_sentence: I like to eat cheese with crackers.
cards.example_es: Me gusta comer queso con galletas.
cards.example_en: I like to eat cheese with crackers.
cards.mnemonic_en: Say cheese and picture a mouse shouting queso as it nibbles the cheesy slice.

cards.definition_en: A pale yellow dairy product made by churning cream, used as a spread or in cooking.
cards.example_sentence: I like to spread butter on the bread.
cards.example_es: Me gusta untar mantequilla en el pan.
cards.example_en: I like to spread butter on the bread.
cards.mnemonic_en: A buttered bat spreads mantequilla on toast, melting like gold.

cards.definition_en: A sweet spread made from fruit and sugar, typically used on bread or toast.
cards.example_sentence: I like spreading jam on toast every morning.
cards.example_es: Me gusta untar mermelada en el pan tostado cada mañana.
cards.example_en: I like spreading jam on toast every morning.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a traffic jam of strawberries spreading mermelada on toast.

cards.definition_en: A sweet, crystalline substance used to sweeten foods and drinks, derived from sugarcane, sugar beet, or other plants.
cards.example_sentence: I need to buy sugar for the recipe.
cards.example_es: Necesito comprar azúcar para la receta.
cards.example_en: I need to buy sugar for the recipe.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine sweet sugar sprinkling like 'azúcar' dust on a cake.

cards.definition_en: A white crystalline mineral used as a seasoning and preservative.
collocations: take it with a grain of salt
cards.example_sentence: Add a pinch of salt to the soup.
cards.example_es: Añade una pizca de sal a la sopa.
cards.example_en: Add a pinch of salt to the soup.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a sea of salt where the word sal splashes onto the plate.

cards.definition_en: A pungent spice made from the dried fruit of the pepper plant, commonly used to season food.
cards.example_sentence: Add a pinch of pepper to the stew.
cards.example_es: Añade una pizca de pimienta al guiso.
cards.example_en: Add a pinch of pepper to the stew.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a pepper shaker spilling bright red pimienta onto a sizzling steak.

cards.definition_en: A solid form of water that is frozen, often used to chill drinks or preserve food.
cards.example_sentence: Put the ice in the drink.
cards.example_es: Pon el hielo en la bebida.
cards.example_en: Put the ice in the drink.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture icy eyes staring at a cold 'hielo' cube melting in a glass.

cards.definition_en: A small piece of cloth or paper used for wiping the mouth or hands while eating.
cards.example_sentence: Please pass me the napkin to clean the table.
cards.example_es: Por favor, pásame la servilleta para limpiar la mesa.
cards.example_en: Please pass me the napkin to clean the table.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a napkin as a tiny nap for a kin, like a servilleta covering spilled soup

cards.definition_en: A small, open container, typically with a handle, used for drinking liquids.
cards.example_sentence: I'm going to buy a new cup for my coffee.
cards.example_es: Voy a comprar una taza nueva para mi café.
cards.example_en: I'm going to buy a new cup for my coffee.
cards.mnemonic_en: I picture a cup with the Spanish word taza painted on its rim.

cards.definition_en: A hard, transparent material used for windows, bottles, and other objects.
cards.example_sentence: Put the glass on the table.
cards.example_es: Pon el vaso sobre la mesa.
cards.example_en: Put the glass on the table.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a clear glass that looks just like a Spanish vaso on the table

cards.definition_en: A flat dish used for serving or eating food.
cards.example_sentence: I placed the plate on the table.
cards.example_es: Puse el plato sobre la mesa.
cards.example_en: I placed the plate on the table.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture Plato serving ideas on a shining plate

cards.definition_en: A utensil with a shallow bowl and a handle used for scooping, stirring, or serving food.
cards.example_sentence: I need a spoon to stir the soup.
cards.example_es: Necesito una cuchara para mezclar la sopa.
cards.example_en: I need a spoon to stir the soup.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a shiny spoon acting as a tiny Cuchara scooping soup.

cards.definition_en: A utensil with two or more prongs used for eating or serving food.
cards.example_sentence: Can you pass me the fork, please?
cards.example_es: ¿Puedes pasarme el tenedor, por favor?
cards.example_en: Can you pass me the fork, please?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a fork as a ten-eh-door opening to scoop up food.

cards.definition_en: A cutting instrument with a blade and handle used for slicing, cutting, or stabbing.
cards.example_sentence: I need a knife to cut the bread.
cards.example_es: Necesito un cuchillo para cortar el pan.
cards.example_en: I need a knife to cut the bread.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a knife slicing a cuckoo, because cuchillo sounds like cuckoo

cards.definition_en: A flat, shallow container used for holding or carrying items.
cards.example_sentence: I placed the food on the tray.
cards.example_es: Puse la comida en la bandeja.
cards.example_en: I placed the food on the tray.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a tray wrapped in a bandage, a real bandeja.

cards.definition_en: A piece of furniture with a flat top and legs, used for working, eating, or placing items.
cards.example_sentence: I placed the book on the table.
cards.example_es: Puse el libro sobre la mesa.
cards.example_en: I placed the book on the table.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a wooden table shaped like a flat mesa in the desert, supporting plates.

cards.definition_en: A piece of furniture with a seat, back, and legs, used for sitting.
cards.example_sentence: The chair is next to the table.
cards.example_es: La silla está junto a la mesa.
cards.example_en: The chair is next to the table.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a chair with a seal (cilla) perched, cheering you to sit.

cards.definition_en: A list of dishes offered by a restaurant or a set of selectable options in a software program.
cards.example_sentence: Can you pass me the menu, please?
cards.example_es: ¿Puedes pasarme el menú, por favor?
cards.example_en: Can you pass me the menu, please?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a menu where the word men serve you tacos.

cards.definition_en: A flat surface in a shop, restaurant, or bank where transactions are conducted.
cards.example_sentence: I left my bag on the store counter.
cards.example_es: Dejé mi bolso en el mostrador de la tienda.
cards.example_en: I left my bag on the store counter.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a shop counter with a most rad sign flashing the price.

cards.definition_en: An outdoor space adjoining a building, often paved and used for dining or recreation.
cards.example_sentence: We sat on the patio to have coffee.
cards.example_es: Nos sentamos en la terraza a tomar el café.
cards.example_en: We sat on the patio to have coffee.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a patio built on a Spanish terraza, the word 'pat' slapping the 'io' tiles.

cards.definition_en: To order food or drink to be taken away and consumed elsewhere.
cards.example_sentence: I want the sandwich to go.
cards.example_es: Quiero el sándwich para llevar.
cards.example_en: I want the sandwich to go.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a waiter shouting Go while handing you a para llevar bag.

cards.definition_en: Used to indicate that something should be placed or remain at this location.
collocations: Is that for here or to go?
Do you want it for here?
Can I get that for here?
cards.example_sentence: For here or to go?
cards.example_es: ¿Para aquí o para llevar?
cards.example_en: For here or to go?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a floor mat that reads FOR right here

cards.definition_en: A statement expressing a desire to place an order.
collocations: I want to order a pizza
synonyms_en: I'd like to place an order
cards.example_sentence: I want to order a pizza for dinner.
cards.example_es: Quiero pedir una pizza para la cena.
cards.example_en: I want to order a pizza for dinner.
cards.mnemonic_en: I hand the chef a huge menu that reads 'quiero-pedir', shouting I want to order.

cards.definition_en: A polite way to say that you want something.
cards.example_sentence: I would like a coffee, please.
cards.example_es: Quisiera un café, por favor.
cards.example_en: I would like a coffee, please.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a key (qui) opening a menu, prompting you to say I would like a...

cards.definition_en: A polite request asking for a suggestion or advice.
collocations: What do you recommend for dinner?
What do you recommend we do?
What do you recommend we try?
cards.example_sentence: What do you recommend for dinner?
cards.example_es: ¿Qué recomienda para la cena?
cards.example_en: What do you recommend for dinner?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a waiter asking, "What do you recommend?" while holding a menu titled RECOMIENDA.

cards.definition_en: A question asking about the contents of something.
collocations: Can you tell me what is in it?
I wonder what is in it.
Do you know what is in it?
Let's find out what is in it.
cards.example_sentence: What is in it?
cards.example_es: ¿Qué lleva la ensalada?
cards.example_en: What is in it?
cards.mnemonic_en: Ask what is in it and hear a chef shout Que lleva about the ingredients.

cards.definition_en: A polite inquiry asking whether vegetarian dishes are available.
collocations: Do you have vegetarian options on the menu?
Do you have vegetarian options available?
Do you have vegetarian options here?
synonyms_en: Can I get a vegetarian meal?
cards.example_sentence: Do you have vegetarian options?
cards.example_es: ¿Tiene opciones vegetarianas?
cards.example_en: Do you have vegetarian options?
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a teen (tiene) asking, 'Do you have vegetarian options?' at a restaurant.

cards.definition_en: A polite inquiry asking if lactose-free milk is available.
cards.example_sentence: Excuse me, do you have lactose-free milk?
cards.example_es: Disculpe, ¿tiene leche sin lactosa?
cards.example_en: Excuse me, do you have lactose-free milk?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a lactose‑free milk saying Do you have to a Spanish shopper

cards.definition_en: A question asking whether something contains no sugar.
cards.example_sentence: "Without sugar?" the waiter asked.
cards.example_es: ¿Sin azúcar? preguntó el camarero.
cards.example_en: "Without sugar?" the waiter asked.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a tiny person shouting wee toss sugar as they drop the sweet, reminding you of without sugar.

cards.definition_en: Indicates that something is served or prepared together with ice.
cards.example_sentence: I want my drink with ice.
cards.example_es: Quiero mi bebida con hielo.
cards.example_en: I want my drink with ice.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cone of ice, con ice, as you say with ice

cards.definition_en: Having a high temperature or feeling warmth.
cards.example_sentence: The coffee is hot.
cards.example_es: El café está caliente.
cards.example_en: The coffee is hot.
cards.mnemonic_en: The hot stove sizzles like a cali‑ENTE chili.

cards.definition_en: Having a low temperature; not warm.
cards.example_sentence: It's very cold today.
cards.example_es: Hace mucho frío hoy.
cards.example_en: It's very cold today.
cards.mnemonic_en: Cold feels like a fridge full of fríó ice cubes.

cards.definition_en: Having a moderately high temperature that is comfortably heated, but not hot.
cards.example_sentence: The coffee is warm, it's neither cold nor hot.
cards.example_es: El café está tibio, no está ni frío ni caliente.
cards.example_en: The coffee is warm, it's neither cold nor hot.
cards.mnemonic_en: A warm sweater feels tibio, like a gentle hug on a cool day.

cards.definition_en: Having a size that is greater than normal or expected.
cards.example_sentence: The building is very large.
cards.example_es: El edificio es muy grande.
cards.example_en: The building is very large.
cards.mnemonic_en: Think of a L‑shaped arch holding a huge grand grande sign.

cards.definition_en: Of intermediate size, amount, or quality.
cards.example_sentence: The dog is medium-sized.
cards.example_es: El perro es de tamaño mediano.
cards.example_en: The dog is medium-sized.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a medium pizza called mediano on the menu.

cards.definition_en: Having a limited size, amount, or extent.
cards.example_sentence: The car is small.
cards.example_es: El coche es pequeño.
cards.example_en: The car is small.
cards.mnemonic_en: A small snail whispers 'pequeño' as it hides in a leaf.

cards.definition_en: Of a size that is larger than large; very big.
cards.example_sentence: I bought an extra large pizza for the party.
cards.example_es: Compré una pizza extra grande para la fiesta.
cards.example_en: I bought an extra large pizza for the party.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a giant extra-large shirt that says extra grande on a runway.

cards.definition_en: A single serving or amount of food.
cards.example_sentence: I want to order one portion of paella for dinner.
cards.example_es: Quiero pedir una ración de paella para cenar.
cards.example_en: I want to order one portion of paella for dinner.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a single ration, a tiny ración of food on a plate.

cards.definition_en: One of two equal parts of a whole.
cards.example_sentence: I ate half a sandwich.
cards.example_es: Comí medio sándwich.
cards.example_en: I ate half a sandwich.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine cutting a medio pizza in half.

cards.definition_en: Complete; not divided or lacking any part.
cards.example_sentence: I ate a whole apple.
cards.example_es: Comí una manzana entera.
cards.example_en: I ate a whole apple.
cards.mnemonic_en: Think of an entire (entero) pizza, whole with no slices missing.

cards.definition_en: A polite way of asking if there is any additional request or information.
collocations: Anything else you want to add?
cards.example_sentence: Anything else?
cards.example_es: ¿Algo más?
cards.example_en: Anything else?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a waiter asking Anything else? while handing you a bag labeled ALGO MAS.

cards.definition_en: It means that there is nothing more; that is everything.
cards.example_sentence: That is all, thank you for your attention.
cards.example_es: Eso es todo, gracias por su atención.
cards.example_en: That is all, thank you for your attention.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a complete set (todo) and you declare, "That is all".

cards.definition_en: A phrase used when handing something to someone.
cards.example_sentence: Here you go, your coffee.
cards.example_es: Aquí tiene su café.
cards.example_en: Here you go, your coffee.
cards.mnemonic_en: Hand a key to a teen and say, Here you go.

cards.definition_en: Immediately; without delay.
cards.example_sentence: I will get to the office right away.
cards.example_es: Llegaré a la oficina en seguida.
cards.example_en: I will get to the office right away.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a right arrow that instantly 'in segue' to the next scene, meaning right away.

cards.definition_en: A statement informing someone that their order has been prepared and is now available.
collocations: Your order is ready for pickup
Your order is ready to go
Your order is ready, thank you
cards.example_sentence: Your order is ready to be picked up.
cards.example_es: Su pedido está listo para ser recogido.
cards.example_en: Your order is ready to be picked up.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a your order wearing a ready badge while a Spanish waiter says Su pedido está listo

cards.definition_en: A unique identifier assigned to a purchase or request.
cards.example_sentence: Could you tell me your order number, please?
cards.example_es: ¿Podrías decirme tu número de pedido, por favor?
cards.example_en: Could you tell me your order number, please?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a delivery person handing you an order (pedido) sheet with a big number (número) badge.

cards.definition_en: A vehicle with an open cargo area at the rear used for transporting goods.
collocations: pick up where we left off
cards.example_sentence: The pickup of the package will be tomorrow at 10.
cards.example_es: La recogida del paquete será mañana a las 10.
cards.example_en: The pickup of the package will be tomorrow at 10.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture yourself picking up a big recogida bag of groceries.

cards.definition_en: Quiet and restrained; not inclined to share thoughts or feelings openly.
cards.example_sentence: The seat next to the window is reserved for first-class passengers.
cards.example_es: El asiento junto a la ventana está reservado para los pasajeros de primera clase.
cards.example_en: The seat next to the window is reserved for first-class passengers.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a quiet librarian locking a book in a reserved, secret 'reservado' shelf.

cards.definition_en: Not costing any money; available without payment.
cards.example_sentence: I am free to choose my own path.
cards.example_es: Soy libre de elegir mi propio camino.
cards.example_en: I am free to choose my own path.
cards.mnemonic_en: Free feels like libre—see a bird escaping a cage, shouting Libre!

cards.definition_en: Being used or taken by someone; not free.
cards.example_sentence: I am occupied all day with the project.
cards.example_es: Estoy ocupado todo el día con el proyecto.
cards.example_en: I am occupied all day with the project.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a busy office where every desk is occupied, just like the Spanish word ocupado.

cards.definition_en: to take a seat.
cards.example_sentence: Please, sit down on the chair.
cards.example_es: Por favor, siéntate en la silla.
cards.example_en: Please, sit down on the chair.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine sending a seat to someone, then you sit down.

cards.definition_en: to remain in place or stay inactive until something happens
cards.example_sentence: Please wait here until I return.
cards.example_es: Por favor, espera aquí hasta que regrese.
cards.example_en: Please wait here until I return.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture yourself waiting as an esper mind‑reader checks the timer.

cards.definition_en: A row of people or objects arranged one after another.
cards.example_sentence: There is a long line in front of the bank.
cards.example_es: Hay una larga cola frente al banco.
cards.example_en: There is a long line in front of the bank.
cards.mnemonic_en: A line looks like a long cola (tail) stretching forward.

cards.definition_en: A person or organization that purchases goods or services from a business.
cards.example_sentence: The customer requested a detailed invoice.
cards.example_es: El cliente pidió una factura detallada.
cards.example_en: The customer requested a detailed invoice.
cards.mnemonic_en: The customer sounds like client and hands a receipt to the cliente.

cards.definition_en: A person who serves food and drinks to customers in a restaurant or café.
cards.example_sentence: The server brought the check quickly.
cards.example_es: El camarero trajo la cuenta rápidamente.
cards.example_en: The server brought the check quickly.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a server handing a tray to a smiling camaro driver, the camarero.

cards.definition_en: A person who prepares and serves coffee and other drinks, typically in a coffee shop.
cards.example_sentence: The barista made a perfect cappuccino.
cards.example_es: El barista preparó un cappuccino perfecto.
cards.example_en: The barista made a perfect cappuccino.

cards.definition_en: A machine used in stores to record sales transactions and hold cash.
cards.example_sentence: The cash register ran out of paper.
cards.example_es: La caja registradora se quedó sin papel.
cards.example_en: The cash register ran out of paper.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cash register as a giant caja that registers every cash coin.

cards.definition_en: A sum of money given in exchange for goods, services, or to settle a debt.
cards.example_sentence: The payment will be made tomorrow.
cards.example_es: El pago se realizará mañana.
cards.example_en: The payment will be made tomorrow.
cards.mnemonic_en: Think of a PAYing MENTor handing a coin for each PAGO you settle.

cards.definition_en: A small piece of paper or plastic used for identification, payment, or playing games.
cards.example_sentence: I need my credit card to pay the bill.
cards.example_es: Necesito mi tarjeta de crédito para pagar la cuenta.
cards.example_en: I need my credit card to pay the bill.
cards.mnemonic_en: I picture a card shaped like a tiny tarjeta with a car driving on it.

cards.definition_en: Money in the form of coins and banknotes that is readily available for transactions.
cards.example_sentence: I prefer to pay in cash.
cards.example_es: Prefiero pagar en efectivo.
cards.example_en: I prefer to pay in cash.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a cash register shouting Efectivo as it prints money.

cards.definition_en: the act or result of making something different, a transformation or alteration.
cards.example_sentence: The change in weather affects me.
cards.example_es: El cambio de clima me afecta.
cards.example_en: The change in weather affects me.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cashier handing you a fresh stack of bills, a cambio, to remember Change.

cards.definition_en: A small amount of money given to someone as a gratuity for their service.
cards.example_sentence: I left a good tip for the waiter.
cards.example_es: Dejé una buena propina al camarero.
cards.example_en: I left a good tip for the waiter.
cards.mnemonic_en: Tip the waiter with a tiny propina, like sprinkling a pinch of salt.

cards.definition_en: A written acknowledgment that something, typically a payment, has been received.
cards.example_sentence: I kept the receipt of the purchase so I could return the item.
cards.example_es: Guardé el recibo de la compra para poder devolver el artículo.
cards.example_en: I kept the receipt of the purchase so I could return the item.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a receipt with the bold word Recibo printed on it.

cards.definition_en: A bill that is issued separately from other charges.
cards.example_sentence: Could you bring me the separate bill, please?
cards.example_es: ¿Podrías traerme la cuenta separada, por favor?
cards.example_en: Could you bring me the separate bill, please?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a separate bill, a split cuenta left on the table.

cards.definition_en: Asking if it is possible to make a payment now.
collocations: Can I pay now or later?
Can I pay now with a credit card?
Can I pay now and get a receipt?
synonyms_en: Is it possible to pay now?
Can I settle the bill now?
cards.example_sentence: Can I pay now?
cards.example_es: ¿Puedo pagar ahora?
cards.example_en: Can I pay now?
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a tin can paying a bill right now, shouting 'Can I pay now?' like a Spanish shopper.

cards.definition_en: A polite way to ask if a business accepts credit or debit cards.
cards.example_sentence: Do you take cards?
cards.example_es: ¿Aceptan tarjeta?
cards.example_en: Do you take cards?
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a shopkeeper holding a deck, asking Accept an card? Do you take cards?

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that an item or element is absent.
collocations: something is missing in the report
cards.example_sentence: Something is missing from the song; it doesn't sound complete.
cards.example_es: Falta algo en la canción; no suena completa.
cards.example_en: Something is missing from the song; it doesn't sound complete.
cards.mnemonic_en: A faulty gap appears where the word 'falta' signals something is missing

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that there is no onion.
cards.example_sentence: I would like the pizza without onion, please.
cards.example_es: Quisiera la pizza sin cebolla, por favor.
cards.example_en: I would like the pizza without onion, please.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a No Onion sign with a sin wave spilling over a bowl (cebula).

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that there is no milk.
cards.example_sentence: I want coffee with no milk.
cards.example_es: Quiero café sin leche.
cards.example_en: I want coffee with no milk.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a cow holding a sign that reads No milk, because it’s sin (without) any dairy.

cards.definition_en: An increased amount of water.
cards.example_sentence: I would like more water, please.
cards.example_es: Quiero más agua, por favor.
cards.example_en: I would like more water, please.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a thirsty dog shouting más agua for more water.

cards.definition_en: A second napkin, used as a replacement or additional one.
collocations: spill the sauce, get another napkin
cards.example_sentence: Can you pass me another napkin, please?
cards.example_es: ¿Puedes pasarme otra servilleta, por favor?
cards.example_en: Can you pass me another napkin, please?
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a second napkin, because 'another napkin' sounds like 'otra napkin' in Spanish.

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that the table is not clean.
cards.example_sentence: The table is dirty.
cards.example_es: La mesa está sucia.
cards.example_en: The table is dirty.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a table covered in mud, just like a sucia mesa.

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that something tastes very good.
cards.example_sentence: The cake is delicious.
cards.example_es: El pastel está delicioso.
cards.example_en: The cake is delicious.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a chef tasting soup and saying It is delish-ous while pointing at the menu that reads ESTA.

cards.definition_en: A statement indicating that the temperature is high.
cards.example_sentence: The coffee is very hot.
cards.example_es: El café está muy caliente.
cards.example_en: The coffee is very hot.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture the sun shouting Caliente as the air becomes very hot.

cards.definition_en: A polite phrase said to someone before they start eating, wishing them a pleasant meal.
cards.example_sentence: Enjoy your meal!
cards.example_es: ¡Buen provecho!
cards.example_en: Enjoy your meal!
cards.mnemonic_en: When you prove a chew, you say enjoy your meal.

title: English C1 Vocabulary
description: Advanced English words and expressions for upper-level learners.

cards.definition_en: to make something clear or easier to understand
cards.example_sentence: Could you clarify the steps we need to follow to complete the project?
cards.example_es: ¿Podrías aclarar los pasos que debemos seguir para completar el proyecto?
cards.example_en: Could you clarify the steps we need to follow to complete the project?
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a clear window, 'a clear' view, to clarify the meaning.

cards.definition_en: To give special importance or attention to something.
cards.example_sentence: The teacher wanted to emphasize the importance of punctuality.
cards.example_es: El profesor quiso recalcar la importancia de la puntualidad.
cards.example_en: The teacher wanted to emphasize the importance of punctuality.
cards.mnemonic_en: A RECALL CAR EMphasizes the road sign, so you never miss the point.

cards.definition_en: To keep something in its existing state or condition.
cards.example_sentence: It is important to maintain a balanced diet to stay healthy.
cards.example_es: Es importante sostener una dieta equilibrada para mantenerse saludable.
cards.example_en: It is important to maintain a balanced diet to stay healthy.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a sturdy stand (sostener) that you maintain, never letting the banner fall.

cards.definition_en: To invite someone to compete or to question the validity of something.
cards.example_sentence: It's important to challenge the sources before believing them.
cards.example_es: Es importante cuestionar las fuentes antes de creer en ellas.
cards.example_en: It's important to challenge the sources before believing them.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a chalice smashing a question mark—challenge means to cuestionar.

cards.definition_en: To provide evidence or proof that something is true or valid.
cards.example_sentence: The researcher must substantiate his conclusions with solid data.
cards.example_es: El investigador debe respaldar sus conclusiones con datos sólidos.
cards.example_en: The researcher must substantiate his conclusions with solid data.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a submarine planting a sturdy stance on a ship's back to support it

cards.definition_en: To reject, disregard, or send away someone or something.
cards.example_sentence: The judge decided to dismiss the case due to lack of evidence.
cards.example_es: El juez decidió desestimar el caso por falta de pruebas.
cards.example_en: The judge decided to dismiss the case due to lack of evidence.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a missed call being ignored, a dis-miss that dismisses the message.

cards.definition_en: To encourage or promote the development or growth of something.
cards.example_sentence: The government wants to foster technological innovation in the country.
cards.example_es: El gobierno quiere impulsar la innovación tecnológica en el país.
cards.example_en: The government wants to foster technological innovation in the country.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a foster parent giving a child a strong push, a force that impulsar forward.

cards.definition_en: To involve or require something as a necessary consequence.
cards.example_sentence: This project entails a great financial risk.
cards.example_es: Este proyecto conlleva un gran riesgo financiero.
cards.example_en: This project entails a great financial risk.
cards.mnemonic_en: A tangled tail (en-tail) carries the load, just like conllevar means to involve

cards.definition_en: To make something less severe, harmful, or painful.
cards.example_sentence: The government implemented new policies to mitigate the effects of climate change.
cards.example_es: El gobierno implementó nuevas políticas para mitigar los efectos del cambio climático.
cards.example_en: The government implemented new policies to mitigate the effects of climate change.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a tiny mite gently soothing a blaze, so the fire is mitigated.

cards.definition_en: To make a problem, situation, or feeling worse or more intense.
cards.example_sentence: Lack of sleep can exacerbate the symptoms of depression.
cards.example_es: La falta de sueño puede agravar los síntomas de la depresión.
cards.example_en: Lack of sleep can exacerbate the symptoms of depression.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture an ex‑ace chef adding extra sour sauce, making the dish even worse.

cards.definition_en: To propose or suggest something for consideration.
cards.example_sentence: The committee decided to put forward a new strategy for the next year.
cards.example_es: El comité decidió plantear una nueva estrategia para el próximo año.
cards.example_en: The committee decided to put forward a new strategy for the next year.
cards.mnemonic_en: Plant a seed and put forward your idea, just like you would plantear a new garden.

cards.definition_en: To direct attention to, discuss, or deal with something.
cards.example_sentence: The committee must address the climate change problem as soon as possible.
cards.example_es: El comité debe abordar el problema del cambio climático lo antes posible.
cards.example_en: The committee must address the climate change problem as soon as possible.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a fancy dress boarding a ship, so you address (abordar) the problem.

cards.definition_en: To find out or establish something through investigation, calculation, or reasoning.
cards.example_sentence: Scientists must determine the cause of the outbreak.
cards.example_es: Los científicos deben determinar la causa del brote.
cards.example_en: Scientists must determine the cause of the outbreak.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a judge stamping a verdict, saying determine as if shouting the Spanish word determinar.

cards.definition_en: Including all or nearly all elements or aspects; thorough and complete.
cards.example_sentence: The report is very comprehensive and covers all aspects of the project.
cards.example_es: El informe es muy comprensivo y cubre todos los aspectos del proyecto.
cards.example_en: The report is very comprehensive and covers all aspects of the project.
cards.mnemonic_en: A COMPREHENSIVE textbook feels like a comprensivo hug that covers every detail.

cards.definition_en: Having subtle distinctions or variations in meaning, expression, or tone.
cards.example_sentence: His argument is nuanced and considers several points of view.
cards.example_es: Su argumento es matizado y considera varios puntos de vista.
cards.example_en: His argument is nuanced and considers several points of view.
cards.mnemonic_en: New ants add subtle shades to the painting, making it nuanced.

cards.definition_en: Open to more than one interpretation or having an unclear meaning.
cards.example_sentence: The message of the ad was ambiguous, so many interpreted it in different ways.
cards.example_es: El mensaje del anuncio era ambiguo, por lo que muchos lo interpretaron de distintas maneras.
cards.example_en: The message of the ad was ambiguous, so many interpreted it in different ways.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a vague sign that reads ambi‑guo, reminding you the meaning is ambiguous

cards.definition_en: Logical, consistent, and forming a unified whole.
cards.example_sentence: His argument is coherent and easy to follow.
cards.example_es: Su argumento es coherente y fácil de seguir.
cards.example_en: His argument is coherent and easy to follow.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a coherent story where every line fits, just like the Spanish word coherente snapping neatly together.

cards.definition_en: Exact and accurate, leaving no room for error or ambiguity.
cards.example_sentence: I need a precise watch to measure the race time.
cards.example_es: Necesito un reloj preciso para medir el tiempo de la carrera.
cards.example_en: I need a precise watch to measure the race time.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a precise surgeon shouting preciso while making exact, razor‑thin cuts.

cards.definition_en: Evoking interest, attention, or admiration in a powerful and persuasive way.
cards.example_sentence: The lawyer's argument was so compelling that the jury had no doubts.
cards.example_es: El argumento del abogado fue tan convincente que el jurado no tuvo dudas.
cards.example_en: The lawyer's argument was so compelling that the jury had no doubts.
cards.mnemonic_en: A compelling speaker convinces the crowd like a confident convict shouting convince

cards.definition_en: A difference or inconsistency between two or more things that should be the same.
cards.example_sentence: There is a large discrepancy between what the witness said and the evidence.
cards.example_es: Hay una gran discrepancia entre lo que dijo el testigo y la evidencia.
cards.example_en: There is a large discrepancy between what the witness said and the evidence.
cards.mnemonic_en: A DISCO and a PANCake on the same plate look mismatched, a clear discrepancy.

cards.definition_en: Something that is accepted as true without proof.
cards.example_sentence: The assumption that the project will be profitable is optimistic.
cards.example_es: El supuesto de que el proyecto será rentable es optimista.
cards.example_en: The assumption that the project will be profitable is optimistic.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a chef who assumes a sup of soup, and that guess becomes the assumption.

cards.definition_en: A way of dealing with a problem or task.
cards.example_sentence: The project's approach was approved by the committee.
cards.example_es: El planteamiento del proyecto fue aprobado por el comité.
cards.example_en: The project's approach was approved by the committee.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a plant reaching out as your approach to a problem

cards.definition_en: A basic structure or system that supports the development or organization of something.
cards.example_sentence: The framework of the theory allows understanding the basic concepts.
cards.example_es: El marco de la teoría permite entender los conceptos básicos.
cards.example_en: The framework of the theory allows understanding the basic concepts.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine Marco the builder erecting a sturdy wooden frame, a literal framework for his house.

cards.definition_en: A particular way of regarding something; a point of view.
cards.example_sentence: From my perspective, the project has a lot of potential.
cards.example_es: Desde mi perspectiva, el proyecto tiene mucho potencial.
cards.example_en: From my perspective, the project has a lot of potential.
cards.mnemonic_en: See a pair of spectacles shifting your view—just like the Spanish perspectiVA.

cards.definition_en: A piece of information discovered as a result of investigation or research.
cards.example_sentence: The archaeologist's finding changed the understanding of the civilization.
cards.example_es: El hallazgo del arqueólogo cambió la comprensión de la civilización.
cards.example_en: The archaeologist's finding changed the understanding of the civilization.
cards.mnemonic_en: Finding a hall a‑go, I discovered the hidden treasure.

cards.definition_en: Evidence is information or objects that support a claim, belief, or conclusion.
cards.example_sentence: The evidence suggests that the suspect was at the crime scene.
cards.example_es: La evidencia sugiere que el sospechoso estaba en la escena del crimen.
cards.example_en: The evidence suggests that the suspect was at the crime scene.
cards.mnemonic_en: Think of Eve's dance on a courtroom floor as the evidence.

cards.definition_en: A limitation or restriction that controls what can be done or achieved.
cards.example_sentence: The speed constraint on the highway is 100 km/h.
cards.example_es: La restricción de velocidad en la autopista es de 100 km/h.
cards.example_en: The speed constraint on the highway is 100 km/h.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a restric-tion rope tightening, turning a box into a strict constraint.

cards.definition_en: Possible to do or achieve; capable of being carried out successfully.
cards.example_sentence: The project is feasible if we secure more funding.
cards.example_es: El proyecto es viable si conseguimos más financiación.
cards.example_en: The project is feasible if we secure more funding.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a feasible plan as a feast in a viable garden, showing it can be done.

cards.definition_en: Capable of being maintained over the long term without depleting resources or causing harm.
cards.example_sentence: This company has implemented sustainable practices to reduce its carbon footprint.
cards.example_es: Esta empresa ha implementado prácticas sostenibles para reducir su huella de carbono.
cards.example_en: This company has implemented sustainable practices to reduce its carbon footprint.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a sturdy table that can sustain, its label reads SOSTENIBLE, showing it's sustainable.

cards.definition_en: Describing something that provokes public disagreement or debate.
cards.example_sentence: The issue of euthanasia remains controversial in many countries.
cards.example_es: El tema de la eutanasia sigue siendo controvertido en muchos países.
cards.example_en: The issue of euthanasia remains controversial in many countries.
cards.mnemonic_en: A heated debate turns the article into a contra verse, making it controversial.

cards.definition_en: Existing or occurring over a large area or among many people.
cards.example_sentence: The use of mobile phones has become very widespread throughout the country.
cards.example_es: El uso de los teléfonos móviles se ha vuelto muy generalizado en todo el país.
cards.example_en: The use of mobile phones has become very widespread throughout the country.
cards.mnemonic_en: A wide spread of general news blankets the town, like a generalizado blanket.

cards.definition_en: Being the most common, strongest, or most important element in a group or situation.
cards.example_sentence: The color blue is predominant in the room's decor.
cards.example_es: El color azul es predominante en la decoración de la habitación.
cards.example_en: The color blue is predominant in the room's decor.
cards.mnemonic_en: Imagine a PRE-DOMINANT lion, the king before any other, just like a predominante leader.

cards.definition_en: Existing beneath the surface or forming the basis of something.
cards.example_sentence: The underlying problem is the lack of communication.
cards.example_es: El problema subyacente es la falta de comunicación.
cards.example_en: The underlying problem is the lack of communication.
cards.mnemonic_en: Picture a submarine (sub) hidden under the waves, the underlying secret.

cards.definition_en: To go beyond or rise above something, especially limits, expectations, or ordinary experience.
cards.example_sentence: Van Gogh's art manages to transcend time and continues to inspire new generations.
cards.example_es: El arte de Van Gogh logra trascender el tiempo y sigue inspirando a nuevas generaciones.
cards.example_en: Van Gogh's art manages to transcend time and continues to inspire new generations.
cards.mnemonic_en: Visualize a ladder that trascends the clouds, letting you rise beyond all limits.

cards.definition_en: To weaken or damage something gradually or subtly.
cards.example_sentence: Constant criticism can undermine the team's confidence.
cards.example_es: Las críticas constantes pueden perjudicar la confianza del equipo.
cards.example_en: Constant criticism can undermine the team's confidence.
cards.mnemonic_en: A miner digs under the bridge, causing it to collapse and perjudicar the village.

cards.definition_en: to act in accordance with a rule, request, or demand
cards.example_sentence: It is important to comply with the safety regulations.
cards.example_es: Es importante cumplir con las normas de seguridad.
cards.example_en: It is important to comply with the safety regulations.
cards.mnemonic_en: See a cumulative pile of rules you must comply with.

cards.definition_en: to follow or comply with a rule, guideline, or request
cards.example_sentence: It is important to adhere to the company's rules.
cards.example_es: Es importante ajustarse a las normas de la empresa.
cards.example_en: It is important to adhere to the company's rules.
cards.mnemonic_en: A deer adjusts to the fence, just like you adhere to rules.

cards.definition_en: to cause something to happen or exist
collocations: give rise to a new trend
cards.example_sentence: The new policy can give rise to massive protests.
cards.example_es: La nueva política puede dar lugar a protestas masivas.
cards.example_en: The new policy can give rise to massive protests.
cards.mnemonic_en: A river gives rise to a new path, just like "dar" creates space for something to happen.

cards.definition_en: To consider or regard something when making a decision.
collocations: take into account the fact that
